title: HCDF (Hardware Configuration Descriptive Format)
description: JSON Schema for HCDF documents, auto-generated from hcdf.xsd. The XSD is the source of truth.

span.description: Wing span (tip-to-tip for one wing, or half-span if describing one side). Unit: "m".

chord.description: Mean aerodynamic chord (leading edge to trailing edge). Unit: "m".

profile.description: Airfoil profile designation (e.g., "NACA2412", "NACA0012", "ClarkY", "E387"). Determines lift/drag characteristics.

sweep.description: Wing sweep angle. Positive = swept back. Unit: "deg".

dihedral.description: Wing dihedral angle. Positive = tips higher than root. Affects roll stability. Unit: "deg".

area.description: Wing planform area. Unit: "m2". If omitted, computed from span x chord.

aerofoil_surface.description: Aerodynamic lifting surface (wing, horizontal stabilizer, canard). Generates lift and drag from airflow over an airfoil cross-section. Key parameters determine flight performance: lift coefficient, stall characteristics, and induced drag.

capabilities.description: Wireless capabilities of this antenna (bands, standards, gain, polarization).

pose.description: Physical location and orientation of the antenna on the component as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

geometry.description: Geometry for visualization of the antenna (e.g., a cylinder for a whip antenna).

name.description: HCDF logical name for this antenna. Required. Used in wireless link definitions to identify endpoints.

type.description: Wireless technology type. Required. Determines the valid capabilities and link types.

visual.description: Name of a visual element on this component whose geometry represents this antenna.

mesh.description: URI of a mesh file representing the antenna's physical shape.

antenna.description: Wireless radio antenna on a component. Used for WiFi, Bluetooth, LoRa, cellular, UWB, GNSS, NFC, and IEEE 802.15.4 connections. Antennas are referenced by name in wireless link definitions.

items.description: Supported frequency band (e.g., "2.4GHz", "5GHz", "900MHz", "L1/L2" for GNSS). Multiple band elements for multi-band antennas.

band.description: Supported frequency band (e.g., "2.4GHz", "5GHz", "900MHz", "L1/L2" for GNSS). Multiple band elements for multi-band antennas.

items.description: Wireless standard supported (e.g., "802.11ax", "bluetooth-5.3", "lora-1.0.4"). Multiple standards for multi-standard radios.

standard.description: Wireless standard supported (e.g., "802.11ax", "bluetooth-5.3", "lora-1.0.4"). Multiple standards for multi-standard radios.

items.description: Higher-layer wireless protocol (e.g., "DroneCAN", "MAVLink", "DDS", "Zenoh"). Multiple protocol elements for multi-protocol capability.

protocol.description: Higher-layer wireless protocol (e.g., "DroneCAN", "MAVLink", "DDS", "Zenoh"). Multiple protocol elements for multi-protocol capability.

gain.description: Antenna gain in dBi (decibels relative to isotropic). Higher gain = more directional.

antenna_capabilities.description: Wireless antenna hardware capabilities. Declares supported frequency bands, wireless standards, protocols, gain, and polarization.

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

frequency-range.description: Operating frequency range. Unit: "Hz" or "kHz". Example: min="20" max="20000" unit="Hz" for audible microphone, min="38" max="42" unit="kHz" for 40kHz ultrasonic. Defines the acoustic bandwidth.

sensitivity.description: Microphone sensitivity. Unit: "dBV/Pa" (typical: -44 to -26 dBV/Pa for MEMS microphones). For ultrasonic: transmit SPL in dB at reference distance.

channels.description: Number of acoustic channels. 1 for mono, 2+ for microphone array (used for beamforming and sound localization).

beam-width.description: Acoustic beam width for directional transducers (ultrasonic, sonar). Unit: "deg". Narrower beam = longer range, lower angular coverage.

type.description: Audio sensor type (microphone, ultrasonic, sonar, hydrophone). Required.

active.description: Whether this is an active transducer (emits and receives) or passive (receives only). true for ultrasonic/sonar emitter+receiver pairs; false for microphones and hydrophones.

audio_sensor.description: Audio/acoustic sensor for sound capture, ultrasonic ranging, and underwater acoustics. Can be a passive receiver (microphone, hydrophone) or an active transducer (ultrasonic, sonar) that emits and receives. Extends sensor_base with audio type attribute and optional frequency/directivity parameters.

xyz.description: Axis direction vector as three space-separated doubles "x y z". Does not need to be unit-length (will be normalized). Default: "1 0 0" (X axis). Examples: "0 0 1" for Z-axis rotation, "0 1 0" for Y-axis.

axis.description: Joint axis of rotation or translation. Defines the direction vector along or around which the joint moves, expressed in the joint's local frame.

x.description: Which body-frame axis the sensor's X axis maps to. Default: X (no remapping).

y.description: Which body-frame axis the sensor's Y axis maps to. Default: Y (no remapping).

z.description: Which body-frame axis the sensor's Z axis maps to. Default: Z (no remapping).

axis_align.description: Axis alignment mapping from a sensor's internal coordinate frame to the component's body frame. Used to correct for sensors mounted in non-standard orientations without changing the pose.

chemistry.description: Cell chemistry: "LiFePO4" (3.2V/cell, safest, longest life), "Li-ion" (3.7V/cell, highest energy density), "LiPo" (3.7V/cell, high discharge rate), "NiMH" (1.2V/cell), "lead-acid" (2.0V/cell).

cells-series.description: Number of cells in series (determines voltage: cells x cell_voltage). Example: 8S LiFePO4 = 25.6V nominal.

cells-parallel.description: Number of cells in parallel (multiplies capacity and max current).

nominal-voltage.description: Nominal pack voltage. Unit: "V". Equals cells-series x cell nominal voltage.

capacity.description: Battery capacity. Unit: "Ah" (amp-hours). Energy = capacity x nominal-voltage.

energy.description: Total stored energy. Unit: "Wh" (watt-hours). Can be computed from capacity x voltage but useful to state explicitly.

max-discharge.description: Maximum continuous discharge rate. Unit: "C" (multiples of capacity). Example: 3C on a 12Ah pack = 36A max continuous.

min-voltage.description: Minimum safe pack voltage (low-voltage cutoff). Unit: "V". Discharging below this damages the cells.

max-charge-rate.description: Maximum charge rate. Unit: "C" (multiples of capacity). Example: 1C on 12Ah = 12A max charge current. Fast-charge capable batteries may support 2-4C. Determines minimum charge time: time_hours = 1/charge_rate_C.

charge-protocol.description: Charging protocol: "cc-cv" (constant-current then constant-voltage — standard for Li-ion/LiPo/LiFePO4), "cc" (constant-current only — NiMH, NiCd), "fast-charge" (proprietary fast-charge protocol), "trickle" (low-rate maintenance charge). Determines charger compatibility.

cycle-life.description: Rated cycle life — number of charge/discharge cycles to 80% capacity retention. LiFePO4: 2000-5000 cycles. Li-ion: 300-500. LiPo: 200-300. An agent can estimate remaining battery health.

battery_source.description: Rechargeable battery energy source. Defines cell configuration, chemistry, capacity, and discharge limits. An agent uses this to compute runtime (energy / total power consumption) and validate that bus voltage stays within all consumers' operating range throughout discharge.

size.description: Box dimensions as three space-separated doubles "width depth height" in meters (X Y Z extents).

box.description: Axis-aligned box geometry primitive centered at the parent pose origin.

description.description: Human-readable description of the bus purpose (e.g., "CAN bus for motor controllers", "I2C sensor bus").

bitrate.description: Raw bit rate in bits per second (e.g., 500000 for CAN 500 kbit/s, 1000000 for CAN-FD 1 Mbit/s data phase). Alternative to speed/baud for bus types where bitrate is the standard specification.

speed.description: Bus speed with explicit unit (e.g., 10 with unit="Mbps" for 10BASE-T1S). Alternative to bitrate.

baud.description: Baud rate for UART buses (e.g., 115200 with unit="Bd"). Alternative to bitrate/speed.

items.description: Higher-layer protocol(s) used on this bus (e.g., "DroneCAN", "CANopen", "Modbus-RTU").

protocol.description: Higher-layer protocol(s) used on this bus (e.g., "DroneCAN", "CANopen", "Modbus-RTU").

voltage.description: Bus voltage level in volts (e.g., 3.3 for I2C, 5.0 for CAN). Used for level-shifter planning and power budget.

plca.description: PLCA configuration for 10BASE-T1S Ethernet buses. Only applicable when type="ethernet" and standard="10base-t1s".

items.description: Devices connected to this bus. Each participant identifies its component, port, address, and role.

participant.description: Devices connected to this bus. Each participant identifies its component, port, address, and role.

name.description: Unique name for this bus within the network (e.g., "can_motors", "i2c_sensors", "spi_imus"). Required.

type.description: Bus protocol type. Required. Determines valid participant roles, addressing, and speed parameters.

topology.description: Physical wiring topology (daisy-chain, star, or multidrop). Optional; defaults depend on bus type (CAN is typically multidrop, SPI is typically star).

bus.description: Shared medium network where all participants are electrically connected and see all traffic simultaneously. No per-hop forwarding — latency is the same for all participants. Use for CAN, I2C, SPI, UART (multi-drop), 10BASE-T1S, servo buses (STS/Dynamixel), and power distribution.

items.description: Higher-layer protocol(s) this participant uses on the bus (e.g., "DroneCAN", "Modbus-RTU", "CANopen"). Multiple entries for multi-protocol devices.

protocol.description: Higher-layer protocol(s) this participant uses on the bus (e.g., "DroneCAN", "Modbus-RTU", "CANopen"). Multiple entries for multi-protocol devices.

device.description: Component name of the participant device. Required. Must match a comp name in the HCDF document.

port.description: Port name on the participant device connected to this bus. Required.

position.description: Physical position of this participant on the bus (0-based index). Defines ordering for daisy-chain and star topologies. Optional for multidrop.

id.description: Bus-level device identifier (e.g., CAN node ID, I2C 7-bit address in hex "0x50", Modbus slave address). Format depends on bus type.

address.description: Alternative address format for protocols that use addressing differently from id (e.g., IP address on 10BASE-T1S bus). Use id for bus-native addressing, address for higher-layer addressing.

role.description: Functional role on the bus (controller, target, source, sink). Determines communication patterns. Optional.

terminator.description: Whether this participant provides bus termination resistance. Set true for endpoints of CAN buses and other buses requiring termination. Optional; default: false.

cs.description: SPI chip-select line number for SPI bus participants. Identifies which CS pin the SPI controller uses to select this device. Only applicable for SPI buses.

plca-id.description: PLCA node ID for 10BASE-T1S participants. Determines the transmit opportunity slot in the PLCA cycle. Range 0-254. Only applicable for Ethernet buses with PLCA.

bus_participant.description: A device connected to a shared bus. Identifies the component, port, bus address, and role on the bus. Multiple participants share the same physical medium and see all traffic simultaneously.

fx.description: Calibrated focal length in pixels along X axis. Overrides intrinsics/fx for runtime use.

fy.description: Calibrated focal length in pixels along Y axis. Overrides intrinsics/fy.

cx.description: Calibrated principal point X in pixels. Often differs from image center by several pixels due to lens/sensor alignment.

cy.description: Calibrated principal point Y in pixels.

k3.description: Calibrated radial distortion coefficient k3 (for wide-angle lenses).

reprojection-error.description: RMS reprojection error from the calibration — the quality metric. Unit: "px" (pixels). Values below 0.5 px are excellent; 0.5-1.0 px is good; above 1.0 px suggests the calibration should be redone. An agent can use this to assess confidence in the calibration.

date.description: Date the calibration was performed in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). An agent can flag stale calibrations (e.g., older than 6 months or after a lens change).

temperature.description: Ambient temperature during calibration. Unit: "degC". Focal length varies with temperature due to thermal expansion of the lens and mount — calibrations at 20°C may not be accurate at -10°C or 50°C.

method.description: Calibration method and target used. Examples: "checkerboard-8x6-30mm" (8x6 inner corners, 30mm square size), "charuco-5x7-40mm" (ChArUco board), "apriltag-6x6-50mm", "circle-grid-4x11-asymmetric". Records how the calibration was done for reproducibility.

images.description: Number of calibration images used. More images generally means better coverage of the image plane and more reliable results. Typical: 20-50 images from diverse angles.

camera_calibration.description: Camera calibration results from an actual calibration procedure on a specific unit. Distinct from the design-spec intrinsics/distortion: those are nominal datasheet values, while calibration contains measured values for this particular camera+lens assembly. An agent doing visual servoing, SLAM, or stereo depth must use the calibrated values. If calibration is present, it overrides the design-spec intrinsics and distortion for runtime use.

k1.description: First radial distortion coefficient. Positive = barrel distortion, negative = pincushion.

k3.description: Third radial distortion coefficient (for wide-angle lenses).

camera_distortion.description: Camera lens distortion parameters following the Brown-Conrady model. Used by vision pipelines to undistort images.

fx.description: Focal length in pixels along the X axis (horizontal).

fy.description: Focal length in pixels along the Y axis (vertical).

cx.description: Principal point X coordinate in pixels (typically image_width / 2).

cy.description: Principal point Y coordinate in pixels (typically image_height / 2).

camera_intrinsics.description: Camera intrinsic parameters following the pinhole camera model. Defines image dimensions, pixel format, and focal length/principal point for projection calculations.

shutter.description: Shutter type: "global" (all pixels exposed simultaneously — no motion blur, required for high-speed robotics) or "rolling" (rows exposed sequentially — causes skew/wobble on fast motion, acceptable for slow scenes).

hdr.description: High Dynamic Range capability. true = can capture indoor shadows and outdoor sunlight in one frame. Critical for robots transitioning between lighting conditions.

depth-range.description: For depth/RGBD cameras: usable depth measurement range. min = closest, max = farthest. Unit: "m".

compression.description: Output compression: "raw" (uncompressed, highest bandwidth — 1080p@30fps is ~1.5Gbps), "jpeg" (lossy per-frame), "h264" (lossy inter-frame), "h265" (best compression, ~5-20Mbps for 1080p). Affects network bandwidth requirements.

trigger.description: Trigger mode: "free-running" (camera controls timing), "hardware" (external GPIO triggers capture — needed for multi-camera synchronization), "software" (software command triggers).

ir-cut.description: IR cut filter present. true = blocks infrared (standard color camera). false = passes IR (for night vision with IR illumination or NIR sensing).

spectral-band.description: Infrared spectral band for thermal cameras: "LWIR" (8-14um, most common, uncooled), "MWIR" (3-5um, cooled, higher sensitivity), "SWIR" (1-3um, see through fog/smoke). Not applicable for visible cameras.

netd.description: Noise Equivalent Temperature Difference — thermal camera sensitivity. Lower = better. Unit: "mK". Typical uncooled LWIR: 40-60mK. Cooled MWIR: 15-25mK.

temperature-range.description: Calibrated temperature measurement range for thermal cameras. Unit: "degC". Example: min="-20" max="150" for industrial, min="-40" max="500" for high-temp.

camera_params.description: Camera-specific parameters affecting perception pipeline design, bandwidth planning, and environmental capability. Applies to optical sensors with type="camera" or type="thermal".

radius.description: Capsule radius (cylinder and hemisphere radius) in meters.

length.description: Length of the cylindrical section in meters (excludes hemispherical caps).

capsule.description: Capsule (cylinder with hemispherical caps) geometry primitive, aligned with the Z axis. Total height = length + 2 * radius.

description.description: Human-readable description of the chain's purpose (e.g., "left arm 802.3dm chain", "EtherCAT servo chain").

vlan.description: VLAN tag for all traffic on this chain. Isolates chain traffic from other network traffic.

macsec.description: Per-chain MACsec policy override. If omitted, the network-wide macsec default applies.

cycle-time-us.description: Chain cycle time in microseconds. For switched chains, this defines the Qbv schedule period applied to all hops. For EtherCAT, this is the bus cycle time.

dc.description: EtherCAT Distributed Clock chain-level configuration. Only applicable when type="ethercat".

tunnel.description: Tunnel configuration when this chain's traffic is encapsulated over another chain or link.

items.description: Ordered list of devices in the chain. At least one hop required. XML element order defines the physical chain topology from root (first) to tail (last).

hop.description: Ordered list of devices in the chain. At least one hop required. XML element order defines the physical chain topology from root (first) to tail (last).

name.description: Unique name for this chain within the network (e.g., "left_arm_chain", "ecat_servos"). Required.

type.description: Chain protocol/PHY standard. Required. Determines valid modes and hop configurations.

mode.description: Forwarding mode. Default: "switched" (store-and-forward). Use "pass-through" for EtherCAT and similar protocols where frames are processed in-flight.

topology.description: Physical chain topology. Default: "line" (open-ended). Use "ring" when the tail connects back to the root for redundancy.

chain.description: Bridged daisy-chain network where each device actively forwards frames to the next hop. Two modes: switched (store-and-forward, e.g., TSN Ethernet with per-hop Qbv scheduling) and pass-through (process-on-the-fly, e.g., EtherCAT where each slave processes frames in hardware as they pass). Hop order defines the physical chain topology. Latency accumulates per hop.

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

type.description: Chemical sensor sub-type (gas, ph, humidity). Required.

chemical_sensor.description: Chemical/environmental sensor (gas detector, pH sensor, humidity sensor). Extends sensor_base with all common elements.

pose.description: Pose of the collision geometry relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians). Default: identity.

geometry.description: Geometry primitive or mesh defining the collision boundary. Required.

surface.description: Surface physics properties (friction, restitution, contact) for this collision shape.

name.description: Unique name for this collision shape within the component. Required.

collision.description: Collision geometry for physics simulation and proximity detection. Typically a simplified version of the visual geometry (bounding box, convex hull, or low-poly mesh). Multiple collision elements per component are allowed for complex shapes.

reason.description: Why this pair is disabled: "adjacent" (share a joint), "unreachable" (physically can't collide), "mounted" (always in contact by design). Optional documentation.

collision_pair.description: A pair of components to skip for self-collision checking. Adjacent links, links on opposite sides of the robot, and always-in-contact pairs are common candidates.

rgba.description: Four space-separated floats "R G B A" each in [0, 1]. R=red, G=green, B=blue, A=alpha (1=opaque, 0=transparent). Default: "0 0 0 1" (opaque black).

color.description: RGBA color specification for visual materials.

description.description: Human-readable description of the component (e.g., "NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin compute module", "shoulder actuator assembly").

board.description: Board or hardware platform identifier (e.g., "jetson-agx-orin-64gb", "nxp-s32n79-evb", "custom-motor-driver-v3"). Used for BOM generation and hardware matching.

operating-temp.description: Operating temperature range of this component. Use min and max attributes for the rated limits. Unit: "degC" (default). Example: min="-40" max="85" for automotive grade, min="0" max="70" for commercial grade, min="-40" max="125" for mil-spec. An agent uses this to validate that all components in a system can operate in the target environment. If the robot operates outdoors in winter (-20 degC), every component's operating-temp range must include that temperature. Components without operating-temp are assumed to have no specific constraint (or the constraint is unknown).

inertial.description: Inertial properties (mass, center of mass, inertia tensor) for dynamics simulation.

items.description: Visual representation(s) for 3D rendering. Multiple visuals per component for multi-part models.

visual.description: Visual representation(s) for 3D rendering. Multiple visuals per component for multi-part models.

items.description: Collision geometry for physics simulation. Multiple collision shapes per component for complex bounds.

collision.description: Collision geometry for physics simulation. Multiple collision shapes per component for complex bounds.

items.description: Named coordinate frames attached to this component for sensor mounts, tool points, etc.

frame.description: Named coordinate frames attached to this component for sensor mounts, tool points, etc.

switch.description: Ethernet switch fabric on this component. At most one per component. Ports inside the switch are bridged; ports outside are independent.

items.description: Independent (non-bridged) ports on this component. Not connected through any switch fabric. Used for point-to-point connections and bus interfaces.

port.description: Independent (non-bridged) ports on this component. Not connected through any switch fabric. Used for point-to-point connections and bus interfaces.

items.description: Wireless antennas on this component (WiFi, BLE, GNSS, UWB, etc.).

antenna.description: Wireless antennas on this component (WiFi, BLE, GNSS, UWB, etc.).

items.description: Sensor group(s) on this component. Each sensor element groups related sub-sensors (IMU, camera, etc.).

sensor.description: Sensor group(s) on this component. Each sensor element groups related sub-sensors (IMU, camera, etc.).

items.description: Motor(s) or actuator(s) on this component. Connected to joints via transmission elements.

motor.description: Motor(s) or actuator(s) on this component. Connected to joints via transmission elements.

items.description: Human-machine interface elements (displays, speakers, LEDs, buttons) on this component.

hmi.description: Human-machine interface elements (displays, speakers, LEDs, buttons) on this component.

items.description: Dynamic surfaces (aerofoils, control surfaces, propellers, wheels, tracks) on this component.

surface.description: Dynamic surfaces (aerofoils, control surfaces, propellers, wheels, tracks) on this component.

items.description: Energy storage (batteries, fuel tanks) on this component.

power-source.description: Energy storage (batteries, fuel tanks) on this component.

software.description: Firmware or software identity running on this component. At most one per component.

discovered.description: Runtime discovery state (IP, port, last-seen). Populated by discovery agents, not authored by hand.

items.description: Vendor or domain-specific extension data for this component.

extension.description: Vendor or domain-specific extension data for this component.

name.description: Unique name for this component (e.g., "spine_soc", "left_shoulder", "wheel_fl_motor_driver"). Required. Used in joint parent/child references, port paths, and chain hop references.

role.description: Functional role (sensor, compute, actuator, parent). Optional. Aids tooling in categorizing components for network planning and visualization.

hwid.description: Hardware identifier for matching physical devices during discovery (e.g., serial number, MAC address prefix, or part number). Optional. Used by discovery agents to map physical hardware to HCDF components.

struct-type.description: Structural classification of this component: fuselage, wing, boom, mast, hull, chassis, frame, arm, leg, tail, nacelle, pod. Optional — provides semantic context for the component's role in the robot's body architecture. Used by agents for high-level reasoning about robot morphology.

ip-rating.description: Ingress Protection rating per IEC 60529 (e.g., "IP67", "IP68", "IP54") or NEMA enclosure type (e.g., "NEMA-4X", "NEMA-6P"). The two-digit IP code indicates protection against solids (first digit, 0-6) and liquids (second digit, 0-9K): IP54 = dust-protected + splash-proof. IP65 = dust-tight + water jets. IP67 = dust-tight + temporary immersion (1m, 30min). IP68 = dust-tight + continuous immersion (depth/duration defined by manufacturer). IP69K = dust-tight + high-pressure steam cleaning. An agent uses this to validate that all components meet the environmental requirements for the target deployment (outdoor, underwater, washdown). Components without ip-rating are assumed to have no specific protection (or the rating is unknown). Optional; free-form string to support both IP and NEMA systems.

comp.description: A component representing a rigid body or hardware assembly. Contains physical properties (inertial, visual, collision), hardware interfaces (ports, antennas, sensors, motors), an optional switch (bridging fabric), firmware identity, and runtime discovery state. Equivalent to URDF's link element but extended with networking and cyber infrastructure.

radius.description: Radius of the cone base in meters.

length.description: Height of the cone from base to apex in meters.

cone.description: Solid cone primitive. Base circle of given radius, tapering to a point at the given length along the Z axis. Use for nose cones, drill bits, funnels, pointed housings. Distinct from frustum which is a truncated cone for FOV visualization.

stiffness.description: Contact stiffness in N/m. Higher values create harder contacts with less penetration.

damping.description: Contact damping coefficient in N*s/m. Dissipates energy during contact to reduce bouncing.

contact.description: Contact dynamics parameters for collision response in physics simulation.

items.description: A supported control mode: "position", "velocity", "torque", "force", "current", "pwm". Multiple modes means the motor supports switching between them.

mode.description: A supported control mode: "position", "velocity", "torque", "force", "current", "pwm". Multiple modes means the motor supports switching between them.

control_modes.description: Control interfaces supported by the motor. Determines how an agent can command this actuator: position (servo-like), velocity (speed control), torque/force (direct effort), current (low-level FOC).

chord-ratio.description: Ratio of control surface chord to parent wing/tail chord (0-1). Typical: 0.2-0.3 for ailerons, 0.3-0.5 for flaps.

span-fraction.description: Fraction of parent wing span covered by this control surface (0-1).

deflection.description: Deflection range. min = max deflection in one direction, max = other direction. Unit: "deg". Example: min="-25" max="25".

type.description: Control surface type: "aileron", "elevator", "rudder", "elevon", "flaperon", "flap", "spoiler", "canard", "ruddervator", "v-tail".

control_surface_def.description: Aerodynamic control surface (aileron, elevon, elevator, rudder, flap, spoiler). A hinged portion of a wing or tail that deflects to change aerodynamic forces. The joint connecting this comp defines the hinge axis; this element defines the aerodynamic properties.

max.description: Maximum current the port can source or sink in the specified unit.

current_capability.description: Port current capability. Text content is the rated current value. Used for power delivery sizing.

length.description: Cylinder length (height) along the Z axis in meters.

cylinder.description: Cylinder geometry primitive centered at the parent pose origin, aligned with the Z axis.

bandwidth.description: Raw output data rate. Unit: "Mbps" or "Gbps". Example: 7000 Mbps for 1920x1080 RGB at 30fps uncompressed. This is the maximum bandwidth the sensor can produce.

bandwidth-compressed.description: Compressed output data rate when hardware compression is enabled. Unit: "Mbps". Example: 20 Mbps for H.265 compressed 1080p@30fps. If the sensor outputs compressed data over the network, use this for bandwidth planning instead of raw bandwidth.

frame-size.description: Size of a single output frame/scan. Unit: "bytes". Example: 6220800 for 1920x1080x3 RGB frame. Used with update-rate to calculate bandwidth: frame-size x update-rate = bandwidth.

data_output.description: Sensor data output characteristics for network bandwidth planning. An agent uses this to validate that network links and TSN schedules can carry the sensor's data. Only relevant for high-bandwidth sensors (cameras, lidars, radars) that transmit over the network. Not needed for low-bandwidth sensors (IMU, baro, mag) connected via SPI/I2C.

reference-clock.description: Component name of the device that serves as the DC reference clock for this chain. Typically the first slave after the master. If omitted, the EtherCAT master auto-selects.

dc_chain.description: EtherCAT Distributed Clock configuration at the chain level. Identifies the reference clock source for the entire EtherCAT segment.

mode.description: DC synchronization mode. free-run: no sync. sync0: synchronized to SYNC0 pulse. sync0-sync1: dual-sync for multi-rate processing.

cycle-time-us.description: SYNC0 cycle time in microseconds. Determines the slave's real-time processing period.

shift-time-ns.description: SYNC0 phase shift in nanoseconds relative to the DC reference clock. Used to stagger processing across slaves to avoid simultaneous current draw. Default: 0.

dc_hop.description: EtherCAT Distributed Clock configuration for a single hop (slave device). Defines the synchronization mode and timing parameters for this slave's local clock.

ip.description: Discovered IP address of the component (e.g., "10.0.1.42"). Populated at runtime.

port.description: Discovered service port number (e.g., 8080 for a web interface, 7400 for DDS). Populated at runtime.

last-seen.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the device was last seen online (e.g., "2026-04-05T10:30:00Z"). Populated at runtime by discovery agents.

discovered.description: Runtime discovery state for a component. Populated by device discovery agents (mDNS, LLDP, EtherCAT scan) at runtime. Not typically authored by hand — updated automatically by HCDF tooling after network scanning.

supported.description: Whether the port supports EEE Low Power Idle mode. Default: false. Note: EEE adds wake latency and is typically disabled for TSN ports.

eee_capability.description: IEEE 802.3az Energy Efficient Ethernet capability of a port.

radii.description: Three semi-axis radii as "rx ry rz" in meters. Example: "0.1 0.08 0.15" for an egg-shaped ellipsoid.

ellipsoid.description: Ellipsoid primitive defined by three semi-axis radii. A sphere is a special case where all three radii are equal. Use for approximating heads, torsos, drone fuselages, and other non-uniform rounded shapes. Supported by SDF.

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

type.description: Electromagnetic sensor sub-type (mag, metal_detector, eddy_current, emf). Required.

em_sensor.description: Electromagnetic field sensor (magnetometer, metal detector, eddy current probe, EMF sensor). Extends sensor_base with all common elements (pose, driver, range, resolution, ODR, bandwidth, FIFO, noise, geometry).

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

type.description: Encoder measurement type: incremental (relative, needs homing), absolute (absolute position per revolution or multi-turn), or linear. Required.

principle.description: Physical sensing principle: optical, magnetic, inductive, capacitive, or resistive.

interface.description: Communication interface: quadrature, ssi, biss, endat, spi, analog.

encoder_sensor.description: Position/velocity encoder sensor. Extends sensor_base with encoder-specific attributes: measurement type (incremental, absolute, linear), physical sensing principle (optical, magnetic, inductive, capacitive, resistive), and communication interface (quadrature, SSI, BiSS, EnDat, SPI). Can be mounted on a motor shaft, joint output, wheel, or linear axis. Motors and transmissions reference encoder sensors by name.

vendor.description: EtherCAT vendor ID (hex string, e.g., "0x00000002" for Beckhoff). Matches the ESI VendorId field.

product.description: EtherCAT product code (hex string). Matches the ESI ProductCode field.

revision.description: EtherCAT revision number (hex string). Matches the ESI RevisionNo field for firmware compatibility.

ethercat_identity.description: EtherCAT slave device identity from the ESI (EtherCAT Slave Information) file. Used to match the physical device to its configuration during startup.

domain.description: Namespace or domain identifier for the extension content (e.g., "gazebo", "isaac-sim", "vendor:mycompany", "ci:github-actions"). Required.

version.description: Version of the extension schema for forward compatibility. Optional.

extension.description: Vendor or domain-specific extension container. Allows embedding arbitrary XML content from external domains (e.g., simulation parameters, vendor-specific configurations, CI/CD metadata) without modifying the HCDF core schema. Content is processed with lax validation.

depth.description: Maximum FIFO depth in samples (hardware limit, e.g., 256 for ICM45686, 512 for BMI088). This is the total buffer capacity before overflow.

watermark.description: Watermark level in samples — interrupt fires when this many samples have accumulated. Controls the effective processing rate: effective_rate = ODR / watermark. Example: ODR=3200Hz, watermark=8 → 400Hz interrupt rate, 8 samples per burst.

fifo_config.description: Hardware FIFO (First-In-First-Out) buffer configuration. Many sensors (IMUs, ADCs) collect samples into a hardware FIFO and trigger an interrupt when the watermark level is reached. This reduces interrupt overhead and allows burst processing. Effective update rate = ODR / watermark.

method.description: How fluid exits the tank: "pressurized" (self-pressurizing from stored pressure, passive — e.g., compressed gas through orifice), "regulated" (pressure regulator controls outlet pressure, active or passive mechanical — e.g., hydrogen fuel cell supply), "pumped" (electric or mechanical pump draws fluid — e.g., gasoline fuel pump), "gravity" (gravity-fed, no pressurization — e.g., open agricultural spray tanks).

outlet-pressure.description: Pressure at the tank outlet / consumer input. For regulated systems this is the regulated setpoint; for pressurized systems it decreases as the tank empties. Use min/max to capture the range. Unit: "bar" or "psi". Example: min="1.5" max="3.0" unit="bar" for a regulated hydrogen supply.

max-flow-rate.description: Maximum sustained (continuous) flow rate the delivery system can provide. Unit depends on fluid: "g/s" or "kg/s" for gases, "L/min" or "L/hr" for liquids, "SLPM" (standard liters per minute) for compressed gases at standard conditions. This is the continuous limit — determined by regulator capacity, pump displacement, or orifice size.

peak-flow-rate.description: Peak (burst) flow rate available for short transients. May exceed max-flow-rate for seconds due to accumulator pressure, pump overspeed, or regulator overshoot. Unit: same as max-flow-rate. If not specified, assume peak = max (no burst capability).

regulator.description: Regulation mechanism controlling outlet pressure and/or flow: "passive-mechanical" (spring-loaded diaphragm regulator — simple, no power needed), "solenoid-proportional" (electronically controlled proportional valve — precise, needs power), "solenoid-on-off" (bang-bang valve — simple digital control), "return-line" (fuel pump + return line to tank — common in automotive), "none" (unregulated — pressure drops as tank empties).

flow_characterization.description: Flow characterization for a fuel/gas tank — how fluid exits the tank and at what rate. Without this, a tank is just a container; the flow characterization tells an agent how fast energy can be delivered to consumers. An agent uses this to compute: max power delivery (flow_rate x energy_density), surge capability (peak flow for acceleration transients), and runtime at load (volume x density / operating_flow_rate).

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

type.description: Force sensor sub-type (strain, pressure, torque, load_cell). Required.

force_sensor.description: Force/torque sensor (strain gauge, pressure transducer, torque sensor, load cell). Extends sensor_base with all common elements.

description.description: Human-readable description of the frame's purpose (e.g., "camera mount point", "tool center point").

pose.description: Pose of the frame relative to the component origin (or to the frame named in relative-to) as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

name.description: Unique name for this frame within the component. Required. Referenced by other elements using the frame name.

relative-to.description: Name of another frame that this frame's pose is relative to. If omitted, the pose is relative to the component origin.

type.description: Frame purpose type: "tcp" (Tool Center Point for end-effector control), "sensor-origin" (sensor reference frame), "attachment" (mechanical mounting point), "optical" (camera optical frame, Z-forward convention). Optional — omit for general-purpose frames.

frame.description: Named coordinate frame attached to a component. Used for expressing sensor mounts, tool center points, mounting holes, or any reference point that other elements can relate to via the relative-to attribute.

static.description: Static friction coefficient (dimensionless). Force required to initiate sliding. Default: 0.

dynamic.description: Dynamic (kinetic) friction coefficient (dimensionless). Force during sliding. Must be less than or equal to static. Default: 0.

friction.description: Friction coefficients for contact simulation between surfaces.

near.description: Near plane distance from sensor origin in meters.

far.description: Far plane distance from sensor origin in meters.

fov.description: Full cone angle in radians (for shape="conical"). Defines a circular cross-section FOV.

hfov.description: Horizontal field of view in radians (for shape="pyramidal").

vfov.description: Vertical field of view in radians (for shape="pyramidal").

shape.description: Cross-section shape: "conical" (circular, single fov angle) or "pyramidal" (rectangular, hfov + vfov).

frustum.description: Frustum (truncated cone/pyramid) for sensor field-of-view visualization. Not a solid physics primitive — use cone/box/cylinder for collision. The shape attribute determines the cross-section: "conical" (circular, uses fov angle) or "pyramidal" (rectangular, uses hfov + vfov). Semantic rule: conical requires fov; pyramidal requires hfov + vfov.

type.description: Fuel cell technology: "pem" (Proton Exchange Membrane — most common for robots, uses hydrogen), "dmfc" (Direct Methanol — uses methanol, lower power density), "sofc" (Solid Oxide — high temp, high efficiency, slow startup).

rated-power.description: Continuous rated electrical power output. Unit: "W" or "kW".

peak-power.description: Peak (burst) electrical power output. Unit: "W" or "kW".

efficiency.description: Electrical conversion efficiency as a fraction (0-1). PEM typical: 0.40-0.60. SOFC typical: 0.50-0.65.

output-voltage.description: Output voltage range. Unit: "V". Fuel cell output varies with load — min at max power, max at no load.

fuel_cell.description: Fuel cell energy source — converts chemical fuel (hydrogen, methanol, etc.) directly to electricity through electrochemical reaction. Unlike batteries (stored charge) or tanks (stored fuel for combustion), fuel cells continuously generate electricity as long as fuel is supplied. The tank element should describe the fuel storage; the fuel cell describes the conversion.

tc-mask.description: Bitmask of traffic classes allowed to transmit during this gate interval (e.g., "10000000" opens only TC7, "11111111" opens all TCs). Required. 8-character binary string, one bit per TC (TC7 at left, TC0 at right).

duration-us.description: Duration of this gate interval in microseconds. Required. Semantic rule: all gate durations in a schedule must sum to the schedule's cycle-time-us.

gate.description: A single entry in a Qbv gate control list. Specifies which traffic classes can transmit and for how long during one segment of the repeating schedule cycle.

geometry.description: Geometry container holding exactly one geometric primitive. Solid primitives (box, cylinder, sphere, capsule, cone, ellipsoid, mesh) are used for collision bounds and visual shapes. Frustum is used for sensor field-of-view visualization.

items.description: Supported satellite constellation: GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou, QZSS, NavIC. More = better urban canyon performance.

constellation.description: Supported satellite constellation: GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou, QZSS, NavIC. More = better urban canyon performance.

items.description: Supported frequency band: L1, L2, L5, E1, E5a, E5b, B1, B2. Multi-frequency enables ionospheric correction.

frequency.description: Supported frequency band: L1, L2, L5, E1, E5a, E5b, B1, B2. Multi-frequency enables ionospheric correction.

rtk.description: RTK (Real-Time Kinematic) capable. true = cm-level positioning with base station or NTRIP corrections.

accuracy-standalone.description: Horizontal accuracy without corrections (CEP50). Unit: "m". Typical: 1.5m single-freq, 1.0m multi-freq.

accuracy-rtk.description: Horizontal accuracy with RTK fix. Unit: "m". Typical: 0.01-0.02m.

pps.description: Pulse-Per-Second output for precise time synchronization. Can serve as time reference for gPTP or sensor timestamping.

dead-reckoning.description: Built-in dead reckoning with IMU fusion for GNSS-denied environments (tunnels, urban canyons).

anti-spoofing.description: Anti-spoofing capability. Detects and rejects fake GNSS signals.

gnss_params.description: GNSS receiver-specific parameters defining constellation support, correction capabilities, and positioning accuracy. Applies to rf sensors with type="gnss".

items.description: gPTP clock instances participating in this time domain. One per device-port combination.

clock.description: gPTP clock instances participating in this time domain. One per device-port combination.

port-defaults.description: Default timing parameters for all gPTP ports in this domain.

domain.description: gPTP domain number (0-127). Required. Devices in the same domain synchronize clocks. Multiple domains allow independent time references (e.g., domain 0 for arm TSN, domain 1 for base).

gptp.description: IEEE 802.1AS generalized Precision Time Protocol configuration. Defines the clock domain, grandmaster election priorities, and per-port timing parameters. All TSN scheduling depends on gPTP time synchronization.

device.description: Component name of the device hosting this gPTP clock. Required.

port.description: Port name on the device that participates in gPTP. Required. Must be a port with hw-timestamping capability.

gm-capable.description: Whether this clock can serve as grandmaster. Required. At least one clock in the domain should be gm-capable="true".

priority1.description: BMCA priority1 value (0-255). Lower wins grandmaster election. Default: 128.

priority2.description: BMCA priority2 value (0-255). Tiebreaker after priority1 and clock quality. Default: 128.

clock-class.description: IEEE 1588 clock class indicating accuracy category (6=GPS-locked, 248=default, etc.). Default: 248.

clock-accuracy.description: IEEE 1588 clock accuracy code as hex string. 0x20=25ns, 0x21=100ns, 0xFE=unknown. Default: "0xFE".

gptp_clock.description: gPTP clock instance participating in the IEEE 802.1AS time domain. Each clock represents a device's gPTP-capable port and its grandmaster election parameters.

log-sync-interval.description: Log base-2 of the Sync message interval in seconds. -3 = 125 ms (8 messages/sec), -4 = 62.5 ms. Default: -3.

log-announce-interval.description: Log base-2 of the Announce message interval in seconds. 0 = 1 second. Default: 0.

log-pdelay-req-interval.description: Log base-2 of the Pdelay_Req message interval in seconds for peer delay measurement. 0 = 1 second. Default: 0.

announce-receipt-timeout.description: Number of Announce intervals without receipt before declaring the GM lost. Default: 3.

neighbor-prop-delay-thresh-ns.description: Maximum acceptable neighbor propagation delay in nanoseconds. Links exceeding this threshold are considered not AS-capable. Default: 800 ns.

gptp_port_defaults.description: Default gPTP timing parameters applied to all ports in the domain unless overridden per-port. These values control sync message rates, path delay measurements, and announce timeout.

ref.description: Name of the joint to include in this group.

group_joint_ref.description: Reference to a joint within a group.

ref.description: Name of the group to include as a sub-group.

group_ref.description: Reference to another group (for nested/composite groups).

description.description: Human-readable description of this HMI element.

pose.description: Position and orientation relative to component origin.

geometry.description: Visualization geometry for this HMI element.

name.description: Unique name for this HMI element within the component.

type.description: HMI element type (display, speaker, buzzer, led-status, led-illumination, button, touchscreen, indicator).

visual.description: Name of the visual element containing this HMI's mesh for 3D highlighting.

mesh.description: GLTF mesh node name within the visual for 3D highlighting.

hmi_element.description: Human-Machine Interface element — displays, speakers, buttons, LEDs, and other devices for human interaction or scene manipulation. Unlike sensors (which observe) and motors (which actuate mechanisms), HMI elements communicate with humans or modify the visual/acoustic environment. Examples: status LEDs on a robot body, a speaker for audio alerts, a touchscreen for operator control, IR illumination LEDs for night-vision cameras, an e-stop button.

description.description: Human-readable description of this hop's role in the chain.

dc.description: EtherCAT Distributed Clock configuration for this hop. Only applicable to EtherCAT chains (type="ethercat").

identity.description: EtherCAT slave identity (vendor, product, revision) from the ESI file. Only applicable to EtherCAT chains.

items.description: Branch connections from this hop to devices not in the main chain path.

spur.description: Branch connections from this hop to devices not in the main chain path.

device.description: Component name of the device at this chain position. Required. Must match a comp name in the HCDF document.

port.description: Port name used for the chain connection when the device has a single chain port. Mutually exclusive with port-primary/port-secondary. For simple line topologies.

port-primary.description: Primary chain port name (upstream, toward root). Used for ring topologies or devices with separate ingress/egress ports. Mutually exclusive with port.

port-secondary.description: Secondary chain port name (downstream, toward tail). Used for ring topologies. Mutually exclusive with port.

ingress.description: Ingress port name for devices with asymmetric chain connectivity. Alternative to port-primary for non-ring topologies where ingress and egress are distinct.

egress.description: Egress port name for devices with asymmetric chain connectivity. Alternative to port-secondary for non-ring topologies.

role.description: Role in the chain (root, master, tail). root: chain controller / first hop. master: intermediate forwarding hop. tail: last hop. Optional; inferred from position if omitted.

alias.description: EtherCAT configured station alias. Alternative addressing that persists across topology changes. Only applicable to EtherCAT chains.

position.description: Explicit 0-based position index in the chain. If omitted, position is determined by XML element order. Useful when XML order does not match physical chain order.

fmmu-offset.description: EtherCAT FMMU (Fieldbus Memory Management Unit) logical address offset in bytes. Defines where this slave's process data is mapped in the logical address space. Semantic rule: FMMU offset+size ranges must not overlap across hops.

fmmu-size.description: EtherCAT FMMU mapping size in bytes. Amount of process data (PDOs) mapped for this slave.

processing-delay-ns.description: Processing delay through this hop's switching fabric in nanoseconds. Different switch chips have different forwarding latencies — an NXP SJA1110 may be 3000ns while a simple 2-port bridge MCU may be 1000ns. When specified, the agent uses this instead of a default assumption for per-hop latency computation. If omitted, the agent uses a conservative default based on link speed.

hop.description: A device in a daisy-chain that actively forwards frames to the next device. Each hop represents one store-and-forward bridge (switched mode) or one process-on-the-fly node (pass-through mode). Hop order in the XML defines the physical chain topology from root to tail.

hydrofoil_surface.description: Hydrodynamic lifting surface — generates lift in water. Used on underwater vehicles (AUVs), hydrofoil boats, and submarine planes. Same principles as aerofoil but in water (much higher fluid density).

uri.description: URI of the HCDF file to include (relative to this document or absolute). Required.

sha.description: SHA-256 hash of the included file for content integrity verification. Optional but recommended for reproducible builds.

name.description: Name prefix for all elements from the included file. Prevents name collisions when including the same module multiple times (e.g., "left_arm", "right_arm"). Optional.

pose.description: Pose offset applied to all included components as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians) relative to the including document's world frame. Optional; default: no offset.

include.description: Include reference for model composition. Imports another HCDF file's components, joints, and networks into this document. Used to build complex robots from reusable sub-assemblies (e.g., arm module, leg module, sensor head). The included model's components are merged into this document with optional name prefix and pose offset.

mass.description: Total mass of the component in kilograms (kg).

com.description: Center of mass position as "x y z" in meters relative to the component origin.

inertia.description: Rotational inertia tensor as "ixx ixy ixz iyy iyz izz" in kg*m^2. Six unique components of the symmetric 3x3 inertia matrix about the center of mass.

inertial_properties.description: Inertial properties of a rigid body component. Defines mass, center of mass, and rotational inertia for dynamics simulation. Equivalent to URDF's inertial element.

pose.description: Pose of the inertial sensor relative to the sensor group origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver identification and axis alignment for the inertial sensor IC.

accel.description: Accelerometer measurement parameters (range in g, resolution, noise, bandwidth). Present when type is "accel" or "accel_gyro".

gyro.description: Gyroscope measurement parameters (range in deg/s, resolution, noise, bandwidth). Present when type is "gyro" or "accel_gyro".

geometry.description: Geometry for visualizing the inertial sensor package.

type.description: Inertial sensor sub-type. Required. Determines which parameter sub-elements (accel, gyro) are expected.

inertial_sensor.description: Inertial measurement sensor (accelerometer, gyroscope, or combined IMU). Contains pose, driver identification, and separate parameter blocks for accelerometer and gyroscope axes.

description.description: Human-readable description of the joint's purpose (e.g., "left shoulder pitch", "front-left wheel spin").

parent.description: Parent component of the joint. Required. The parent remains fixed while the child moves.

child.description: Child component of the joint. Required. The child moves relative to the parent.

origin.description: Pose of the joint frame relative to the parent component's origin. Defines where the joint is located and how it is oriented on the parent body.

axis.description: Primary axis of rotation/translation in the joint frame. Required for revolute, continuous, prismatic, universal, and screw joints. Forbidden for fixed joints.

axis2.description: Secondary axis for universal joints (2-DOF). Defines the second rotation axis, which must be perpendicular to the primary axis. Only used when type="universal".

limit.description: Position, velocity, effort, acceleration, and jerk limits. Required for revolute and prismatic joints. Optional for continuous (velocity/effort limits only). Forbidden for fixed.

dynamics.description: Damping, friction, and spring parameters for simulation.

calibration.description: Calibration reference data for homing the joint to an absolute position.

mimic.description: Mimic constraint that makes this joint track another joint's position.

loop.description: Loop closure marker. Present when this joint closes a kinematic loop (parallel mechanism). The kinematic tree parser should treat this joint as a constraint, not a parent-child relationship. Used for four-bar linkages, delta robots, Stewart platforms.

name.description: Unique name for this joint (e.g., "left_shoulder_pitch", "wheel_fl_spin"). Required. Referenced by transmission elements and mimic constraints.

type.description: Joint type determining degrees of freedom and required sub-elements. Required.

thread_pitch.description: Thread pitch for screw joints in meters per revolution. Defines the linear advance per full rotation. Only applicable when type="screw".

joint.description: Kinematic connection between two components defining degrees of freedom. Supports 8 joint types: revolute (bounded rotation), continuous (unbounded rotation), prismatic (linear), fixed (rigid), ball (3-DOF spherical), universal (2-DOF), planar (2D translation), and screw (helical). Semantic rule: revolute/prismatic require axis + limit; fixed forbids them; universal requires axis + axis2.

reference_position.description: Absolute joint position (radians or meters) corresponding to the calibration reference mark. Default: 0.

rising.description: Joint position (radians or meters) at the rising edge of the calibration sensor during a positive-direction sweep.

falling.description: Joint position (radians or meters) at the falling edge of the calibration sensor during a positive-direction sweep.

joint_calibration.description: Joint calibration data for homing and reference position determination. Used to relate encoder readings to absolute joint positions.

comp.description: Name of the child component. Required. Must match a comp name defined in the HCDF document.

joint_child.description: Child component reference in a joint. The child is the component that moves relative to the parent through the joint's degrees of freedom.

damping.description: Viscous damping coefficient. Nm/(rad/s) for revolute, N/(m/s) for prismatic. Default: 0.

friction.description: Coulomb friction torque/force. Nm for revolute, N for prismatic. Applied as constant resistance opposing motion. Default: 0.

spring_stiffness.description: Spring stiffness. Nm/rad for revolute, N/m for prismatic. Creates a restoring force toward spring_reference position. Default: 0 (no spring).

spring_reference.description: Spring rest position. Radians for revolute, meters for prismatic. The position at which the spring exerts zero force. Default: 0.

joint_dynamics.description: Joint dynamics parameters for simulation. Defines viscous damping, Coulomb friction, and optional spring behavior.

description.description: Human-readable description of this group's purpose.

items.description: A joint included in this group.

joint.description: A joint included in this group.

items.description: A sub-group included in this group (hierarchical composition).

group.description: A sub-group included in this group (hierarchical composition).

name.description: Unique name for this group (e.g., "left_arm", "right_hand", "locomotion", "head").

type.description: Group purpose type: "end-effector" (gripper/hand), "kinematic-chain" (arm/leg), "locomotion" (wheels/legs). Optional.

tip-comp.description: For end-effector groups: the component at the tip of the kinematic chain (e.g., the hand palm link). Used for forward kinematics to the end-effector.

tip-frame.description: For end-effector groups: the TCP (Tool Center Point) frame at the tip. References a frame name on the tip-comp.

joint_group.description: Named collection of joints that can be planned and controlled as a unit. Groups enable efficient motion planning (plan for 7 arm joints, not 30+ total), task decomposition ("use left_arm to pick up cup"), and cross-embodiment transfer (different robots both define a "manipulation" group). Groups can contain other groups for hierarchical composition. Equivalent to SRDF joint groups.

lower.description: Lower position limit. Radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic. Default: 0.

upper.description: Upper position limit. Radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic. Default: 0. Semantic rule: upper must be greater than or equal to lower for non-continuous joints.

effort.description: Maximum joint effort (torque for revolute in Nm, force for prismatic in N). Default: 0 (unlimited if not specified by tooling).

velocity.description: Maximum joint velocity (rad/s for revolute, m/s for prismatic). Default: 0 (unlimited if not specified by tooling).

acceleration.description: Maximum joint acceleration (rad/s^2 for revolute, m/s^2 for prismatic). Optional; used for trajectory planning.

jerk.description: Maximum joint jerk (rad/s^3 for revolute, m/s^3 for prismatic). Optional; used for smooth trajectory generation.

joint_limit.description: Position, velocity, effort, acceleration, and jerk limits for a joint. Required for revolute and prismatic joints. Units depend on joint type: radians and Nm for revolute, meters and N for prismatic.

comp.description: Name of the parent component. Required. Must match a comp name defined in the HCDF document.

joint_parent.description: Parent component reference in a joint. The parent is the component that remains "fixed" while the child moves relative to it through the joint's degrees of freedom.

joint.description: Name of the joint this position applies to. Must reference an existing joint.

value.description: Joint position value in radians (revolute, continuous) or meters (prismatic).

joint_position.description: A single joint position value within a named kinematic state. References a joint by name and provides its position in radians (revolute/continuous) or meters (prismatic).

type.description: Credential storage type (secure-element, hsm, tpm, file, runtime). Required.

ref.description: Implementation-specific reference to the key material (e.g., HSM slot ID, TPM handle, file path, or SE key index). Opaque string resolved by the runtime.

key_store.description: Reference to the credential storage backend for MACsec key material. Points the runtime MACsec daemon to the appropriate key source.

description.description: Human-readable description of this state's purpose.

items.description: Position of a joint in this state. One per joint that differs from zero/default.

joint-position.description: Position of a joint in this state. One per joint that differs from zero/default.

name.description: Unique name for this state (e.g., "home", "transport", "calibration", "pre-grasp").

default.description: If true, this is the default state used for visualization, collision checking, and as the starting configuration. At most one state should be default.

kinematic_state.description: Named kinematic state (pose/keyframe) defining a joint configuration. Examples: "home" (standard ready position), "transport" (compact for shipping), "calibration" (known reference for sensor alignment). An AI agent can reference states by name instead of specifying individual joint angles. One state can be marked as default.

scan-type.description: Scanning mechanism: "mechanical" (spinning head — 360° FOV but vibration-sensitive, has wear parts), "solid-state" (no moving parts, limited FOV, very robust), "mems" (MEMS mirror, compact, moderate FOV), "flash" (illuminates entire scene at once, short range, no scanning).

channels.description: Number of vertical channels (laser/detector pairs). More = denser vertical coverage. Typical: 16, 32, 64, 128.

points-per-second.description: Total point measurement rate. Determines point cloud density and downstream processing/bandwidth. Example: 300000 (Livox Mid-360), 1200000 (Hesai XT32).

scan-rate.description: Frame/rotation rate. For mechanical: rotations per second. For solid-state: frame rate. Unit: "Hz". Typical: 10-20.

returns.description: Return mode: "single" (first or strongest only), "dual" (first + strongest), "triple" (first + second + strongest). Multi-return sees through rain, dust, and vegetation.

wavelength.description: Laser wavelength. 905nm = common, lower cost, affected by sunlight. 1550nm = eye-safe at higher power, better rain/fog penetration, more expensive. Unit: "nm".

horizontal-fov.description: Horizontal field of view. 360 for spinning lidars, 60-120 for solid-state. Unit: "deg".

vertical-fov.description: Vertical field of view as min/max angle. Example: min="-25" max="15" unit="deg".

lidar_params.description: Lidar-specific parameters defining scan geometry, point cloud density, and environmental robustness. Applies to optical sensors with type="lidar".

description.description: Human-readable description of the link's purpose (e.g., "compute-to-switch uplink", "WiFi telemetry link").

wired.description: Wired point-to-point link (Ethernet, T1 Ethernet, fiber).

wireless.description: Wireless link (WiFi, Bluetooth, LoRa, cellular, UWB).

name.description: Unique name for this link within the network (e.g., "spine_uplink", "arm_t1", "wifi_telemetry"). Required.

link.description: Point-to-point connection between exactly two ports (wired) or two antennas (wireless). The standard attribute identifies the PHY standard (e.g., 25gbase-sr, 100base-t1, 802.3dm). Can include VLAN assignment, MACsec policy, and IP configuration.

tsn-tlvs.description: Whether the port supports TSN-specific LLDP TLVs for automatic TSN capability discovery and configuration (IEEE 802.1Qcc). Default: false.

lldp_capability.description: IEEE 802.1AB LLDP (Link Layer Discovery Protocol) capability of a port.

predecessor.description: Predecessor body (comp name) in the kinematic loop at this closure point.

successor.description: Successor body (comp name) in the kinematic loop at this closure point.

constraint-axes.description: 6-value string "tx ty tz rx ry rz" where 1=constrained, 0=free. Example: "1 1 1 1 1 0" constrains all except rotation around Z.

loop_closure.description: Marks a joint as closing a kinematic loop. Contains the predecessor/successor bodies and which DOFs are constrained at the closure point. Used by dynamics solvers (HyRoDyn, Drake) to handle parallel mechanisms that URDF's tree-only structure cannot represent.

items.description: A supported MACsec cipher suite. At least one must be listed. Multiple entries indicate the port can negotiate among them.

cipher.description: A supported MACsec cipher suite. At least one must be listed. Multiple entries indicate the port can negotiate among them.

macsec_capability.description: IEEE 802.1AE MACsec hardware capability of a port. Lists the cipher suites the port's MACsec engine can accelerate in hardware.

key-store.description: Credential storage backend for this network's MACsec keys.

policy.description: Default MACsec enforcement policy for all links and chains in this network. Required. Individual links/chains can override with their own macsec element.

cipher.description: Default cipher suite. Default: gcm-aes-128. Can be overridden per link/chain.

key-agreement.description: Default key agreement protocol. Default: psk. Can be overridden per link/chain.

rekey-interval-sec.description: Automatic rekeying interval in seconds. Optional. When set, MACsec associations are rekeyed periodically to limit exposure of compromised keys.

macsec_config.description: IEEE 802.1AE MACsec network-wide default security policy. Individual links and chains can override. No actual keys — only policy intent. Keys are provisioned at runtime via the key-store reference.

policy.description: MACsec enforcement level. Required. must-secure drops unprotected frames; should-secure prefers MACsec; integrity-only authenticates without encrypting; disabled turns MACsec off.

cipher.description: Cipher suite for this link/chain. Default: gcm-aes-128.

key-agreement.description: Key agreement protocol. Default: psk (pre-shared key).

key-ref.description: Reference identifier for the key material in the key store. Opaque string resolved at runtime by the MACsec key agreement daemon.

confidentiality-offset.description: Number of bytes after the SecTAG to leave unencrypted (0, 30, or 50). Default: 0 (encrypt everything). Use 30 to expose L3/L4 headers for hardware QoS classification on non-MACsec-aware switches.

macsec_policy.description: Per-link or per-chain IEEE 802.1AE MACsec security policy. Overrides the network-wide macsec default when present. Specifies policy intent only — actual keys are provisioned at runtime.

description.description: Human-readable description of the material (e.g., "anodized aluminum", "carbon fiber composite").

color.description: RGBA color of the material for visual rendering.

surface.description: Surface physics properties (friction, restitution, contact) when this material is involved in collisions.

name.description: Unique name for this material. Required. Referenced from visual material elements and collision surfaces.

material_global.description: Globally defined material that can be referenced by name from visual and surface elements throughout the HCDF document. Defines appearance (color) and physics (surface properties) in one reusable definition.

color.description: RGBA color for this material. Optional if referencing a global material by name.

name.description: Reference to a globally defined material name (from top-level material elements), or a local name for this inline material definition.

material_inline.description: Inline material definition for a visual element. Either defines color directly or references a globally defined material by name.

unit.description: Physical unit of the measurement. Optional — if omitted, the SI default for the parent element applies. Specify explicitly for non-SI units (e.g., "RPM/V", "deg", "rpm", "g", "dps", "mK") or when the unit is ambiguous.

measured_value.description: A scalar measurement with an optional unit. The text content is the numeric value. HCDF assumes SI units by default — if the unit attribute is omitted, the parent element's documented default SI unit applies (meters for length, kg for mass, radians for angles, seconds for time, Hz for frequency, V for voltage, A for current, W for power, ohm for resistance, Nm for torque, N for force, degC for temperature, Mbps for network speed). Specify unit explicitly only when using a non-SI unit or when the context is ambiguous (e.g., velocity-constant where unit distinguishes Ke from Kv). Example: <speed>100</speed> means 100 Mbps (SI default for speed). <velocity-constant unit="RPM/V">920</velocity-constant> needs explicit unit.

uri.description: URI of the mesh file (relative to the HCDF document or absolute). Required.

scale.description: Scale factors as three space-separated doubles "sx sy sz" applied to the mesh. Default: "1 1 1" (no scaling).

mesh.description: External 3D mesh geometry loaded from a file. Supports GLB, glTF, STL, OBJ, and DAE formats.

device.description: Device (component) name that participates in this mesh. Must reference a comp defined in the HCDF.

antenna.description: Antenna name on the device used for mesh communication. Must reference an antenna defined on the referenced comp.

role.description: Role in the mesh: coordinator (forms and manages the network), border-router (bridges mesh to external network, e.g., Thread border router to Ethernet), router (relays traffic for other nodes), end-device (leaf node, does not relay). Default behavior is protocol-dependent.

id.description: Node identifier within the mesh (e.g., Thread short address, BLE mesh unicast address, UWB node ID). Format is protocol-dependent.

mesh_node.description: A participant in a wireless mesh network. Unlike bus participants (shared medium) or chain hops (ordered forwarding), mesh nodes self-organize their routing topology. The mesh protocol handles path discovery, multi-hop forwarding, and self-healing.

joint.description: Name of the leader joint to mimic. Required. Semantic rule: must reference an existing joint defined in the HCDF document.

multiplier.description: Scaling factor applied to the leader joint's position. Default: 1.0 (identical motion). Use -1 for opposite-direction coupling.

offset.description: Constant offset added after scaling (radians for revolute, meters for prismatic). Default: 0.

mimic.description: Mimic joint constraint. Makes this joint's position track another joint's position via a linear relationship: position = multiplier * leader_position + offset. Used for mechanically coupled joints (e.g., parallel gripper fingers, coupled four-bar linkages).

uri.description: URI of the 3D model file (GLB/glTF format). Relative to the HCDF document or absolute. Required.

sha.description: SHA-256 hash of the referenced file for content integrity verification. Optional but recommended for reproducible builds.

model_ref.description: Reference to an external 3D model file (GLB or glTF) for high-fidelity visual representation. Preferred over geometry primitives when available.

pose.description: Pose of the motor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

voltage.description: Motor voltage ratings with nominal/continuous/peak/max levels. Unit: typically "V".

current.description: Motor current ratings with nominal/continuous/peak/max levels. Unit: typically "A".

torque-constant.description: Motor torque constant (Kt). Torque output per ampere of current. Unit: typically "Nm/A".

velocity-constant.description: Motor velocity constant — the proportionality between voltage and rotational speed. This single element covers both Ke (back-EMF constant) and Kv (speed constant) conventions; the unit attribute determines which: use unit="V/RPM" or unit="V.s/rad" for Ke (back-EMF style, common on industrial servo and hub motor datasheets, e.g., 0.109 V/RPM for ZLLG65ASM250-L); use unit="RPM/V" for Kv (speed constant style, common on drone/RC BLDC datasheets, e.g., 920 RPM/V). Ke and Kv are reciprocals of each other — an agent computes one from the other as needed.

max-speed.description: Maximum no-load shaft speed. Unit: typically "rpm" or "rad/s".

stall-torque.description: Stall torque at rated voltage. Unit: typically "Nm".

thermal-resistance.description: Thermal resistance from winding to ambient. Unit: typically "degC/W". Used for thermal derating calculations.

max-temperature.description: Maximum allowable winding temperature before demagnetization or insulation failure. Unit: typically "degC".

thrust-axis.description: Direction of thrust force as "x y z" unit vector relative to the component frame. Only for type="thrust". Example: "0 0 1" for upward thrust.

max-thrust.description: Maximum thrust force output. Unit: "N". Only for type="thrust".

pole-pairs.description: Number of magnetic pole pairs in the motor. Determines the electrical frequency: electrical_freq = mechanical_rpm × pole_pairs / 60. Critical for BLDC commutation (Hall sensor transitions per revolution = pole_pairs × 6), ESC configuration, and back-EMF frequency. Example: 15 pole pairs for ZLLG65ASM250-L hub motor, 7 for typical drone motor, 2 for industrial servo.

displacement.description: Engine displacement for internal combustion motors. Unit: "cc" (cubic centimeters) or "L" (liters). Only applicable for type="ice". Example: 50cc for a small generator, 0.8L for a robot mower engine.

cylinders.description: Number of cylinders for internal combustion motors. Only applicable for type="ice".

stroke.description: Linear travel range for linear actuators. Unit: "m" or "mm". Defines the maximum extension distance. Only applicable for type="linear".

control-modes.description: Supported control interfaces for this motor.

no-load-speed.description: No-load shaft speed at rated voltage. Unit: "rpm". From motor load test. Higher than rated speed. Example: 257.9 rpm for ZLLG65ASM250-L at 24V.

no-load-current.description: No-load current draw at rated voltage. Unit: "A". The minimum current the motor draws when spinning freely. Important for idle power budget. Example: 0.974A.

max-power-out.description: Maximum mechanical power output from load test. Unit: "W". The peak of the power-vs-torque curve. Example: 158.8W at 11.61 Nm for ZLLG65ASM250-L.

max-efficiency.description: Peak efficiency from load test. Unit: "percent". The best-case power_out/power_in ratio. Example: 68.4% at 3.84 Nm / 216.3 RPM. An agent should target this operating point for best runtime.

name.description: Unique name for this motor within the component (e.g., "shoulder_motor", "wheel_fl"). Required. Referenced by transmission elements.

type.description: Motor technology type (bldc, brushed, stepper, servo, linear, hydraulic, pneumatic, thrust, ice). Optional; useful for selecting appropriate control algorithms.

construction.description: Motor physical construction: "inrunner" (rotor inside stator — higher RPM, common in industrial servos and drone motors), "outrunner" (rotor outside stator — higher torque, common in hub motors and large drone motors), "axial-flux" (flat pancake design — very thin, high torque density), "pancake" (flat form factor). Only relevant for BLDC/brushed motors.

visual.description: Name of the visual element containing this motor's mesh for 3D highlighting.

mesh.description: GLTF mesh node name within the visual for 3D highlighting of this motor.

encoder.description: Name reference to an encoder sensor on this component. Identifies motor-side position feedback. The encoder is a full sensor with its own pose, driver, noise model, and resolution.

hall.description: Name reference to a Hall sensor on this component used for BLDC commutation. Typically an em-type sensor with 3 Hall elements (U/V/W) providing 6-step commutation signals. Often shares the motor signal connector with the encoder.

thermistor.description: Name reference to a temperature sensor on this component monitoring motor winding temperature. Typically an NTC thermistor embedded in the motor stator. Used for thermal protection — the agent can monitor temperature and derate or shut down before exceeding max-temperature.

motor.description: Motor or actuator with electrical, mechanical, and thermal parameters. Voltage and current use rated_value type with nominal/continuous/peak/max ratings. Connected to joints via transmission elements.

description.description: Human-readable description of the network's purpose and scope (e.g., "main TSN backbone", "left arm 802.3dm chain network").

items.description: Point-to-point link connections (wired or wireless) in this network.

link.description: Point-to-point link connections (wired or wireless) in this network.

items.description: Shared-medium bus segments in this network.

bus.description: Shared-medium bus segments in this network.

items.description: Bridged daisy-chain segments in this network.

chain.description: Bridged daisy-chain segments in this network.

items.description: Wireless mesh networks (Thread, BLE mesh, UWB, WiFi mesh) in this network.

mesh.description: Wireless mesh networks (Thread, BLE mesh, UWB, WiFi mesh) in this network.

gptp.description: IEEE 802.1AS gPTP time synchronization configuration for this network.

traffic-classes.description: Traffic class definitions mapping PCP values to queue priorities.
