items.description: IEEE 802.1Qbv Time-Aware Shaper schedule templates for this network.

schedule.description: IEEE 802.1Qbv Time-Aware Shaper schedule templates for this network.

schedule-assignment.description: Bindings of schedule templates to ports and chains.

items.description: References to external stream profile files defining TSN stream requirements.

stream-profile.description: References to external stream profile files defining TSN stream requirements.

macsec.description: Network-wide IEEE 802.1AE MACsec security policy and key store configuration.

eee-policy.description: Network-wide IEEE 802.3az Energy Efficient Ethernet policy. "enabled" allows Low Power Idle; "disabled" keeps ports always active. Typically disabled for TSN networks.

name.description: Unique name for this network (e.g., "backbone", "arm_network", "sensor_bus_network"). Required.

network.description: Unified network description grouping physical topology (links, buses, chains, meshes) and network configuration (gPTP clock sync, TSN traffic classes, Qbv schedules, MACsec security, EEE policy, stream profiles). A robot can have multiple networks for independent domains (e.g., separate arm and base networks, a Thread sensor mesh).

mean.description: Mean of the noise distribution. Default: 0. Units match the sensor's measurement unit.

stddev.description: Standard deviation of the noise distribution. Default: 0. Units match the sensor's measurement unit.

bias-mean.description: Mean of the bias random walk (constant offset that drifts slowly over time). Default: 0.

bias-stddev.description: Standard deviation of the bias random walk. Default: 0.

precision.description: Quantization precision (ADC resolution). Output is rounded to the nearest multiple of this value. Units match the sensor's measurement unit.

type.description: Noise distribution type. Required. Use "gaussian" for realistic sensor simulation, "none" for ideal sensors.

noise.description: Sensor noise model for simulation. Specifies the statistical distribution and parameters applied to ideal sensor readings. For gaussian type: output = ideal + N(mean, stddev) + bias where bias ~ N(bias-mean, bias-stddev). For uniform type: output = ideal + U(mean - stddev, mean + stddev).

pose.description: Pose of the optical sensor module relative to the sensor group origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver identification for the optical sensor (e.g., "oak-d-pro", "rplidar-a2", "vl53l5cx").

geometry.description: Geometry for visualizing the optical sensor housing.

items.description: Field-of-view definition(s). One per sensing cone/pyramid. Stereo cameras have two FoVs; omnidirectional lidars may have one 360-degree FoV.

fov.description: Field-of-view definition(s). One per sensing cone/pyramid. Stereo cameras have two FoVs; omnidirectional lidars may have one 360-degree FoV.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth for network planning. Cameras and lidars generate significant data — an agent uses this to validate TSN schedules and link capacity.

camera-params.description: Camera-specific parameters (shutter, HDR, compression, trigger, thermal specs). Use when type="camera" or type="thermal".

lidar-params.description: Lidar-specific parameters (scan-type, channels, points-per-second, returns, wavelength). Use when type="lidar".

type.description: Optical sensor sub-type (camera, thermal, lidar, tof, optical_flow). Required.

optical_sensor.description: Optical sensor (camera, lidar, ToF, optical flow). Contains one or more field-of-view definitions, each with its own intrinsics, distortion, and noise model.

role.description: PHY master/slave role. Required. master: provides clock reference. slave: recovers clock from master. auto: negotiate at link-up (not recommended for deterministic systems).

phy_config.description: Physical layer configuration for an Ethernet port. Required for single-pair Ethernet links (100BASE-T1, 1000BASE-T1, 802.3dm) where PHY master/slave role must be explicitly configured. Semantic rule: each point-to-point T1 link requires exactly one master and one slave.

max-nodes.description: Maximum number of PLCA nodes this port supports on a single multi-drop segment. Default: 8.

plca_capability.description: IEEE 802.3cg PLCA (Physical Layer Collision Avoidance) capability for 10BASE-T1S multi-drop Ethernet. PLCA provides deterministic medium access by assigning time slots to each node.

coordinator.description: Device and port that serves as the PLCA coordinator (beacon source). Exactly one coordinator per bus segment.

node-count.description: Total number of PLCA nodes on this bus segment. Used to size the transmit opportunity cycle. Should match the number of participants.

burst-count.description: Maximum number of additional frames a node may send in its transmit opportunity after the first frame. 0 = single frame per opportunity.

plca_config.description: IEEE 802.3cg PLCA configuration for 10BASE-T1S multi-drop Ethernet buses. Defines the coordinator, node count, and burst parameters for deterministic medium access.

device.description: Component name of the PLCA coordinator device. Required.

port.description: Port name on the coordinator device connected to this bus. Required.

plca_coordinator.description: PLCA coordinator designation for IEEE 802.3cg 10BASE-T1S multi-drop Ethernet. The coordinator provides the beacon that synchronizes time slot allocation for all nodes on the segment.

capabilities.description: Hardware capabilities of this port (speeds, protocols, TSN features, power). Optional; omit for ports whose capabilities are implied by type (e.g., simple JTAG/SWD debug ports).

pose.description: Physical location of the port connector on the component as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians) relative to the component origin.

geometry.description: Geometry of the port connector for visualization (e.g., a box representing an RJ45 jack).

name.description: HCDF logical name for this port. Required. Used in link, chain, and bus references to identify connection endpoints (e.g., "eth0", "can_bus", "power_in").

iface.description: OS-level network interface name as seen by the device's operating system (e.g., "eth0", "swp0", "can0", "spi0.1"). Used by runtime agents for interface configuration. Optional; defaults to the HCDF name if not specified.

type.description: Physical interface type of this port. Required. Determines the valid capabilities and connection types.

visual.description: Name of a visual element on this component whose geometry represents this port in 3D visualization. Alternative to inline geometry.

mesh.description: URI of a mesh file representing this port's physical connector shape. Alternative to geometry element.

electrical.description: Electrical output type for SIGNAL ports: "push-pull" (drives both high and low — standard for most digital interfaces), "open-drain" (drives low only, requires external pull-up — allows wired-OR/AND with multiple devices on same line, used for interrupt lines, I2C SDA/SCL), "open-collector" (same as open-drain but BJT-based), "tristate" (can be high, low, or high-impedance — used for shared buses). Only relevant for SIGNAL port type. Optional.

port.description: Physical wired connection interface on a component or switch. The name attribute is the HCDF logical identifier used in link/chain references. The iface attribute is the OS-level interface name (e.g., eth0, swp0, can0) as seen by the device for agent configuration. Contains capabilities describing electrical, protocol, TSN, and power characteristics.

speed.description: Symmetric port speed (same TX and RX rate). Mutually exclusive with speed-tx/speed-rx. Example: 1000 with unit="Mbps".

speed-tx.description: Transmit speed for asymmetric ports. Must be paired with speed-rx. Mutually exclusive with speed.

speed-rx.description: Receive speed for asymmetric ports. Must be paired with speed-tx. Mutually exclusive with speed.

bitrate.description: Raw bit rate for non-Ethernet serial ports (CAN, UART, SPI, I2C). Example: 500000 with unit="bps" for CAN 500 kbit/s.

baud.description: Baud rate (symbols per second) for UART ports where baud differs from bitrate. Example: 115200 with unit="Bd".

standard.description: PHY standard this port implements (e.g., "100base-t1", "1000base-t", "802.3dm", "25gbase-sr"). Free-form string matching IEEE 802.3 clause names.

items.description: Higher-layer protocol(s) the port supports (e.g., "EtherCAT", "PROFINET", "CAN-FD", "Modbus-RTU"). Multiple protocol elements indicate multi-protocol capability.

protocol.description: Higher-layer protocol(s) the port supports (e.g., "EtherCAT", "PROFINET", "CAN-FD", "Modbus-RTU"). Multiple protocol elements indicate multi-protocol capability.

phy.description: PHY layer configuration (master/slave role). Required for single-pair Ethernet (T1) ports.

tsn.description: TSN sub-standard capabilities supported by this port's hardware.

macsec.description: MACsec hardware cipher suites supported by this port.

lldp.description: LLDP discovery capability including TSN TLV support.

voltage.description: Voltage range the port can supply or accept (for power ports and PoDL).

current.description: Maximum current the port can source or sink.

power.description: Maximum power the port can deliver or consume.

typical-power.description: Typical power consumption under normal operating conditions. Unit: "W". Used alongside max power for realistic runtime estimation. Max power gives worst-case budget; typical-power gives expected runtime. Example: a motor controller may draw max="120" W during acceleration but typical="35" W during steady operation.

port_capabilities.description: Hardware capabilities of a physical port. Declares data rates, PHY configuration, TSN features, MACsec support, and power delivery characteristics. Elements must appear in order: data, PHY, TSN, power. Semantic rule: use either speed OR speed-tx + speed-rx, not both.

xyz.description: Position as three space-separated doubles "x y z" in meters. Default: "0 0 0" (origin of parent frame).

rpy.description: Orientation as three space-separated Euler angles "roll pitch yaw" in radians (XYZ extrinsic convention). Default: "0 0 0" (no rotation).

quat.description: Orientation as quaternion "x y z w" (Hamilton convention, scalar-last). Optional; if present, overrides rpy.

pose.description: 6-DOF pose (position + orientation) of an element relative to its parent frame. Specify position via xyz and orientation via either rpy (Euler angles) or quat (quaternion). If both rpy and quat are given, quat takes precedence.

max.description: Maximum power the port can source or sink in the specified unit.

power_capability.description: Port power capability. Text content is the rated power value. Used for total power budget calculations.

fuel-cell.description: Fuel cell energy converter (PEM, DMFC, SOFC). Generates electricity from fuel continuously.

name.description: Unique name for this power source.

role.description: Power source role in the system: "primary" (main power source), "backup" (engaged on primary failure), "hot-swap" (can be replaced while system runs on other source), "ups" (uninterruptible — provides power during switchover). An agent uses this for power redundancy analysis.

group.description: Power source group name for failover pairing. Sources in the same group can substitute for each other. Example: group="main" on two hot-swap batteries means either can power the system alone while the other is replaced.

visual.description: Name of the visual element containing this power source's mesh for 3D highlighting.

mesh.description: GLTF mesh node name within the visual for 3D highlighting of this power source.

power_source.description: Energy source on a component. Contains one or more source types (battery, tank, fuel-cell). An agent computes runtime, power budget, and weight impact from this data. The component's POWER port defines the electrical output; the power-source defines the stored energy.

pitch.description: Propeller pitch — theoretical distance advanced per revolution in ideal fluid. Unit: "m" or "in".

direction.description: Rotation direction viewed from above/front: "CW" (clockwise) or "CCW" (counter-clockwise). Critical for torque balancing on multirotors.

prop_surface.description: Propeller surface — generates thrust from rotation. Used on multirotors, fixed-wing aircraft, boats, and underwater vehicles. The associated motor provides torque; the propeller converts it to thrust based on diameter, pitch, and blade geometry.

frequency.description: Operating frequency. 77-79GHz = automotive standard (high resolution). 24GHz = industrial (longer range, lower resolution). Unit: "GHz".

modulation.description: Modulation scheme: "fmcw" (Frequency-Modulated Continuous Wave — automotive standard, measures range + velocity simultaneously) or "pulsed" (traditional, longer range).

azimuth-fov.description: Horizontal field of view. Unit: "deg". Typical: 120 short-range, 20 long-range.

elevation-fov.description: Vertical field of view (4D imaging radar). Unit: "deg". Presence indicates elevation-capable radar.

azimuth-resolution.description: Azimuth angular resolution — minimum angle to separate two targets horizontally. Unit: "deg".

range-resolution.description: Range resolution — minimum distance to separate two targets at the same angle. Unit: "m". Determined by chirp bandwidth.

velocity-resolution.description: Velocity resolution — minimum speed difference to separate two targets. Unit: "m/s".

max-detections.description: Maximum simultaneous target detections per frame.

mimo.description: MIMO antenna configuration as "NtxMrx" (e.g., "3tx4rx" = 12 virtual channels). More virtual channels = better angular resolution.

radar_params.description: Radar-specific parameters for 4D imaging radar and distance/velocity sensing. Applies to rf sensors with type="radar".

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

type.description: Radiation sensor type (geiger, scintillation, neutron, dosimeter). Required.

radiation_sensor.description: Radiation sensor for detecting ionizing radiation. Used on nuclear inspection robots, space robots, and environmental monitoring platforms. Extends sensor_base with radiation type attribute.

unit.description: Physical unit. Optional — SI default applies if omitted.

min.description: Minimum acceptable value in the specified unit.

max.description: Maximum acceptable value in the specified unit.

range_value.description: A value with minimum and maximum bounds and an optional unit. The text content is the nominal value. Used for port voltage ranges, temperature operating ranges, and similar bounded quantities. SI defaults apply when unit is omitted (V for voltage, degC for temperature, etc.).

unit.description: Physical unit. Optional — V for voltage elements, A for current elements by SI default.

nominal.description: Nominal (design-point) operating value. The steady-state value the motor is designed for.

continuous.description: Maximum continuous value the motor can sustain indefinitely without overheating.

peak.description: Peak value the motor can sustain for a short duration (typically seconds to minutes) before thermal limits are reached.

max.description: Absolute maximum value. Exceeding this causes permanent damage or triggers hardware protection.

rated_value.description: A value with multiple rating levels for motor electrical parameters. Text content is the primary value. Used for motor voltage (default unit: V) and current (default unit: A) where different operating regimes apply (nominal steady-state, continuous thermal limit, short-term peak, absolute maximum). SI defaults apply when unit is omitted.

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

radar-params.description: Radar-specific parameters (frequency, modulation, FOV, resolution, velocity, MIMO). Use when type="radar".

gnss-params.description: GNSS-specific parameters (constellations, frequencies, RTK, accuracy, PPS). Use when type="gnss".

type.description: RF sensor sub-type (gnss, uwb, radar, radio_altimeter). Required.

rf_sensor.description: Radio-frequency sensor (GNSS receiver, UWB ranger, radar, radio altimeter). Extends sensor_base with type-specific parameter blocks for radar and GNSS.

items.description: Ordered list of gate control entries. At least one required. Executed cyclically in XML order.

gate.description: Ordered list of gate control entries. At least one required. Executed cyclically in XML order.

name.description: Unique name for this schedule template (e.g., "arm_1ms_schedule", "base_schedule"). Required. Referenced by schedule-assignment elements.

cycle-time-us.description: Total cycle duration in microseconds. Required. All gate durations must sum to this value. Typical values: 250 (4 kHz), 500 (2 kHz), 1000 (1 kHz).

schedule.description: IEEE 802.1Qbv Time-Aware Shaper gate control list template. Defines a repeating cycle of gate states that open/close transmission gates per traffic class. Each gate entry specifies which traffic classes can transmit and for how long. Semantic rule: gate durations must sum to cycle-time-us.
Name of the schedule template to apply. Required. Must match a schedule name defined in this network.

ports.description: Space-separated list of dotted port references ("comp.port") to apply this schedule to. Optional.

chains.description: Space-separated list of chain names to apply this schedule to (all hops in the chain). Optional.

schedule_assign.description: Assignment of a named schedule template to specific ports and/or chains. Binds a Qbv gate control list to the hardware ports that should execute it.

items.description: Individual schedule assignment binding a template to ports and/or chains.

assign.description: Individual schedule assignment binding a template to ports and/or chains.

schedule_assignment.description: Container for all schedule-to-port/chain assignments in the network. Groups schedule_assign entries that bind Qbv templates to physical ports.

items.description: A pair of components to disable collision checking between.

pair.description: A pair of components to disable collision checking between.

self_collision_disable.description: List of component pairs to skip for self-collision checking. Dramatically improves motion planning performance — typically 80%+ of all link pairs can be skipped. Equivalent to SRDF disable_collisions.

items.description: Inertial measurement unit(s) — accelerometers and/or gyroscopes.

inertial.description: Inertial measurement unit(s) — accelerometers and/or gyroscopes.

items.description: Electromagnetic sensor(s) — magnetometers, metal detectors, eddy current probes.

em.description: Electromagnetic sensor(s) — magnetometers, metal detectors, eddy current probes.

items.description: Optical sensor(s) — cameras, lidars, ToF sensors, optical flow sensors.

optical.description: Optical sensor(s) — cameras, lidars, ToF sensors, optical flow sensors.

items.description: Radio-frequency sensor(s) — GNSS receivers, UWB rangers, radars, radio altimeters.

rf.description: Radio-frequency sensor(s) — GNSS receivers, UWB rangers, radars, radio altimeters.

items.description: Chemical/environmental sensor(s) — gas detectors, pH sensors, humidity sensors.

chemical.description: Chemical/environmental sensor(s) — gas detectors, pH sensors, humidity sensors.

items.description: Force/torque sensor(s) — strain gauges, pressure transducers, torque sensors, load cells.

force.description: Force/torque sensor(s) — strain gauges, pressure transducers, torque sensors, load cells.

items.description: Position/velocity encoder(s) — motor-side, joint-side, wheel, or linear. Optical, magnetic, inductive, capacitive, or resistive principle.

encoder.description: Position/velocity encoder(s) — motor-side, joint-side, wheel, or linear. Optical, magnetic, inductive, capacitive, or resistive principle.

items.description: Temperature sensor(s) — thermistors, RTDs, thermocouples, IR. Common: motor winding temp, battery cell temp, ambient.

temperature.description: Temperature sensor(s) — thermistors, RTDs, thermocouples, IR. Common: motor winding temp, battery cell temp, ambient.

items.description: Radiation sensor(s) — Geiger counters, scintillation detectors, neutron sensors, dosimeters.

radiation.description: Radiation sensor(s) — Geiger counters, scintillation detectors, neutron sensors, dosimeters.

items.description: Audio/acoustic sensor(s) — microphones, ultrasonic transducers, sonar, hydrophones.

audio.description: Audio/acoustic sensor(s) — microphones, ultrasonic transducers, sonar, hydrophones.

items.description: Tactile/contact sensor(s) — capacitive arrays, FSR pads, piezoelectric skins, barometric patches, optical gel sensors. Provides spatially distributed pressure/contact data for grasp planning, slip detection, and safe interaction.

tactile.description: Tactile/contact sensor(s) — capacitive arrays, FSR pads, piezoelectric skins, barometric patches, optical gel sensors. Provides spatially distributed pressure/contact data for grasp planning, slip detection, and safe interaction.

name.description: Unique name for this sensor group within the component (e.g., "imu_main", "front_camera_suite"). Required.

update-rate.description: Sensor output update rate in Hz. Applies to all sub-sensors in this group unless overridden. Optional.

sensor.description: Sensor hardware container grouped by physical measurement category. Contains sub-elements for inertial (accel, gyro), electromagnetic (mag), optical (camera, lidar, tof, optical flow), RF (gnss, uwb, radar), chemical (gas, ph, humidity), force (strain, pressure, torque, load cell), encoder (position/velocity feedback), temperature (thermistor, RTD, thermocouple, IR), radiation (geiger, scintillation, neutron, dosimeter), audio (microphone, ultrasonic, sonar, hydrophone), and tactile (capacitive, resistive, piezoelectric, barometric, optical) sensors. Each sub-sensor has pose, driver identification, measurement parameters, and noise model.

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

sensor_base.description: Base sensor type with elements common to ALL sensor subtypes: pose, driver, range, resolution, noise, data output, and geometry. Extended by em, rf, chemical, force, and encoder sensor types. ODR/bandwidth/FIFO are NOT included here — those are specific to high-rate inertial sensors and live in sensor_params (accel/gyro blocks).

axis-align.description: Axis remapping from sensor internal axes to component body frame. Optional; omit if sensor axes match body frame.

name.description: Driver identifier string used by the runtime to load the correct sensor driver (e.g., "bmi088", "icm42688p", "bmp390"). Required.

sensor_driver.description: Sensor driver identification and configuration. Maps the physical sensor to a software driver by name and provides axis alignment correction for the driver's coordinate frame.

description.description: Human-readable description of this FoV (e.g., "left stereo camera", "forward-facing depth").

pose.description: Pose of the FoV origin relative to the sensor origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians). For stereo cameras, this encodes the baseline offset.

geometry.description: FoV volume geometry for visualization. Typically a frustum (conical or pyramidal).

intrinsics.description: Design-spec camera intrinsic parameters (focal length, principal point, image size) from the datasheet. For calibrated values, use the calibration element instead.

distortion.description: Design-spec lens distortion coefficients (Brown-Conrady model). For calibrated values, use the calibration element instead.

calibration.description: Measured calibration results for this specific camera unit. Contains calibrated intrinsics (fx, fy, cx, cy), distortion coefficients (k1-k3, p1-p2), reprojection error, calibration date, temperature, and method. When present, agents should use these values instead of the design-spec intrinsics/distortion.

noise.description: Per-pixel noise model for this FoV (e.g., depth noise for ToF sensors).

name.description: Unique name for this FoV within the sensor (e.g., "left", "right", "depth", "rgb"). Required.

color.description: Visualization color for the FoV cone in the 3D viewer (e.g., "0.2 0.8 0.2 0.3" for translucent green RGBA).

sensor_fov.description: Field-of-view definition for an optical sensor. Each FoV represents a distinct sensing cone/pyramid (e.g., a stereo camera has two FoVs). Contains the FoV geometry, design-spec intrinsics, distortion model, calibration results, and noise parameters. The intrinsics/distortion elements hold nominal datasheet values; the calibration element holds measured values from an actual calibration on this specific unit. If calibration is present, agents should use it over the design specs.

range.description: Full-scale measurement range (e.g., 16 with unit="g" for +/-16g accelerometer, 2000 with unit="dps" for gyroscope).

resolution.description: Measurement resolution — smallest detectable change (e.g., 0.488 with unit="mg" for 16-bit at 16g range).

odr.description: Output Data Rate — hardware sampling frequency in Hz. This is how fast the ADC converts, regardless of how often software processes the data. Example: 3200 with unit="Hz". Determines Nyquist frequency (odr/2) and aliasing behavior.

bandwidth.description: Anti-aliasing low-pass filter cutoff frequency in Hz. Determines the useful signal bandwidth. Typically at or below ODR/2 (Nyquist). Example: 1600 with unit="Hz" for ODR=3200Hz. Lower bandwidth reduces noise but limits frequency response.

fifo.description: Hardware FIFO buffer configuration. When present, the sensor collects samples at ODR rate into a buffer and interrupts at ODR/watermark rate. Example: ODR=3200Hz, fifo watermark=8 → 400Hz interrupt rate delivering 8 samples per burst.

noise.description: Noise model for this measurement axis. Includes spectral density (stddev), bias instability (bias-mean, bias-stddev), and quantization precision.

sensor_params.description: Measurement parameters for a sensor axis or channel. Defines the operating range, resolution, sampling rate (ODR), anti-aliasing filter bandwidth, FIFO configuration, and noise model. ODR and bandwidth are distinct: ODR is the hardware sampling rate, bandwidth is the LPF cutoff frequency. The effective processing rate depends on ODR and FIFO watermark.

version.description: Firmware or software version string (e.g., "1.2.3", "v2024.03-rc1"). Semantic versioning recommended.

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the firmware binary for integrity verification and update tracking.

firmware-manifest-uri.description: URI of the firmware manifest file describing the full firmware package contents, dependencies, and update instructions.

params.description: Runtime configuration parameters string passed to the firmware at boot (e.g., DTS overlay name, boot arguments).

name.description: Firmware or software package name (e.g., "zephyr-flight-controller", "linux-jetson-orin"). Required.

software.description: Firmware or software identity for a component. Used for firmware manifest tracking, version management, and over-the-air update planning.

sphere.description: Sphere geometry primitive centered at the parent pose origin.

description.description: Human-readable description of the spur connection purpose.

vlan.description: VLAN tag for traffic on this spur link.

device.description: Component name of the device connected via this spur. Required.

port.description: Port name on the spur device that connects to the chain hop. Required.

via.description: Port name on the chain hop device that connects to the spur device. Required. This port must be on the hop device (not in the main chain path).

spur.description: Spur connection from a chain hop to a device not in the main chain path. Represents a branch connection where a chain hop's switch port bridges to an external device (e.g., a sensor or actuator hanging off a chain node via an additional Ethernet port).

uri.description: URI of the stream profile file (relative to the HCDF document or absolute). Required.

sha.description: SHA-256 hash of the referenced stream profile file for content integrity verification.

active.description: Whether this stream profile is currently active. Default: true. Set false to define but not activate a profile (e.g., for A/B switching).

stream_profile_ref.description: Reference to an external stream profile definition file (.streams.xml). Stream profiles define individual TSN streams (talker, listener, bandwidth, latency requirements) separately from the HCDF topology to allow independent versioning and reuse across robots.

restitution.description: Coefficient of restitution (bounciness). 0 = perfectly inelastic (no bounce), 1 = perfectly elastic (full bounce). Dimensionless, range [0, 1].

contact.description: Contact dynamics parameters (stiffness, damping) for physics simulation.

material.description: Reference to a named global material that provides default surface properties. Inline friction/restitution/contact elements override the referenced material's values.

surface.description: Surface physics properties for collision geometry. Defines friction, restitution (bounciness), and contact dynamics used by physics simulators.

aerofoil.description: Aerodynamic lifting surface — wing, stabilizer, canard.

control-surface.description: Aerodynamic control surface — aileron, elevator, rudder, elevon, flap.

name.description: Unique name for this surface within the component.

visual.description: Name of the visual element containing this surface's mesh for 3D highlighting.

mesh.description: GLTF mesh node name within the visual for 3D highlighting of this surface.

surface_element.description: Dynamic surface that interacts with the environment (air, water, or ground). Includes aerodynamic surfaces (wings, control surfaces, propellers), hydrodynamic surfaces (hydrofoils), and ground contact surfaces (wheels, tracks). Each surface has a name and contains exactly one surface type definition. The comp's joints define the actuation (hinge axes, deflection); the surface defines the physical interaction properties.

items.description: Ports bridged by this switch fabric. These ports can forward frames to each other through the switch ASIC.

port.description: Ports bridged by this switch fabric. These ports can forward frames to each other through the switch ASIC.

tsn.description: TSN capabilities of the switch ASIC (Qbv, CBS, PSFP, FRER, preemption).

macsec.description: MACsec hardware cipher suites supported by the switch ASIC.

chip.description: Switch ASIC identifier (e.g., "sja1110", "mv88e6393x", "s32n79-integrated"). Omit for software bridges (Linux bridge, OVS). Optional.

switch.description: Ethernet switching fabric that bridges ports together. Ports inside switch are connected by the switching ASIC and can forward frames between each other. Ports directly on comp (outside switch) are independent. The chip attribute identifies the switch IC (e.g., sja1110, s32n79-integrated). Omit chip for software bridges.

items.description: A MACsec cipher suite supported by the switch ASIC.

cipher.description: A MACsec cipher suite supported by the switch ASIC.

switch_macsec.description: MACsec hardware capabilities of a switch fabric. Lists cipher suites supported by the switch ASIC's MACsec engine for all bridged ports.

qbv.description: IEEE 802.1Qbv Time-Aware Shaper capability at the switch level.

cbs.description: IEEE 802.1Qav Credit-Based Shaper capability at the switch level.

psfp.description: IEEE 802.1Qci Per-Stream Filtering and Policing capability at the switch level.

frer.description: IEEE 802.1CB Frame Replication and Elimination for Reliability capability at the switch level.

preemption.description: IEEE 802.1Qbu/802.3br frame preemption capability at the switch level.

switch_tsn.description: TSN capabilities of a switch fabric (as opposed to per-port capabilities). Declares which TSN sub-standards the switch ASIC supports globally across all its bridged ports.

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

rows.description: Number of rows in the taxel array. Defines the spatial layout along one axis of the sensing surface. Example: 4 rows x 4 cols = 16 taxels in a fingertip pad.

cols.description: Number of columns in the taxel array. Together with rows, defines the rectangular grid of sensing elements.

taxels.description: Total number of individual tactile sensing elements (taxels). For regular grids, taxels = rows x cols. For irregular layouts (e.g., curved fingertip, hexagonal packing), specify taxels directly when rows/cols do not apply. Example: 160 taxels on a SynTouch BioTac, 32 on a XELA uSkin patch.

spatial-resolution.description: Center-to-center spacing between adjacent taxels. Unit: "mm". Determines the minimum feature size the sensor can resolve through touch. Example: 2.5 mm for a fingertip array, 10 mm for a body-skin patch. Smaller spacing = finer spatial detail but more wiring and processing.

pressure-range.description: Measurable contact pressure range per taxel. Unit: "kPa" or "N". min = minimum detectable pressure (sensitivity threshold), max = saturation pressure. Example: min="0.1" max="100" unit="kPa" for a capacitive fingertip. Light touch is ~1 kPa, firm grip is 10-50 kPa, maximum grip can exceed 100 kPa.

sensing-area.description: Total active sensing area of the tactile surface. Unit: "mm2" or "cm2". Example: 314 mm2 for a circular fingertip pad (20mm diameter), 2500 mm2 for a 50x50 palm patch.

type.description: Tactile sensor technology type (capacitive, resistive, piezoelectric, barometric, optical). Required. Determines the sensing principle and typical characteristics: capacitive and barometric are best for static pressure, piezoelectric for dynamic events (slip, texture), optical for rich 3D contact geometry.

tactile_sensor.description: Tactile sensor for distributed contact/pressure measurement across a surface. Unlike force sensors (single-point measurement), tactile sensors provide spatially distributed pressure data across an array of sensing elements (taxels). Used on robot fingertips, palms, grippers, and body skin for grasp planning, slip detection, object recognition by touch, and safe physical interaction. Extends sensor_base with tactile-specific parameters: array dimensions, taxel count, spatial resolution, and pressure range.

fuel.description: Fuel type: "gasoline", "diesel", "hydrogen", "compressed-air", "nitrogen", "propane", "ethanol", "jet-a". Determines energy density and handling requirements.

pressure.description: Storage pressure (for compressed gases). Unit: "bar" or "psi". Example: 350 bar for hydrogen, 200 bar for compressed air.

flow.description: Flow characterization — how fluid exits the tank and at what rate. Defines delivery method (pressurized, regulated, pumped, gravity), outlet pressure, max/peak flow rates, and regulation mechanism. Without this, the tank is just a capacity number with no delivery information.

tank_source.description: Fuel/gas tank energy source. For combustion engines, hydrogen fuel cells, compressed air systems, or pneumatic actuators. Defines fuel type, volume, pressure, energy content, and flow characterization (how fluid exits and at what rate). An agent needs both capacity (how much energy is stored) and flow (how fast it can be delivered) to compute runtime and power capability.

pose.description: Pose of the sensor relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians).

driver.description: Driver IC identification and axis alignment configuration.

noise.description: Noise model for the sensor output.

data-output.description: Output bandwidth and frame size for network planning. Only needed for sensors that transmit significant data over the network (cameras, lidars, radars).

type.description: Temperature sensor type: thermistor (NTC/PTC, common in motors), rtd (PT100/PT1000, high accuracy), thermocouple (K/J/T type, wide range), ir (non-contact infrared). Required.

temperature_sensor.description: Temperature sensor — thermistor, RTD, thermocouple, or IR. Common use: motor winding temperature monitoring (NTC thermistor in motor signal connector), ambient temperature, battery cell temperature. Extends sensor_base with temperature sensor type attribute.

ground-pressure.description: Ground pressure (vehicle weight / total track contact area). Unit: "kPa". Lower = better soft terrain performance.

track_surface.description: Tracked vehicle surface (tank treads, rubber tracks). Defines the track geometry and ground contact properties. Used for high-traction vehicles on rough terrain.

tc.description: Traffic class number (0-7). Required. Maps to hardware transmit queues.

pcp.description: Priority Code Point (0-7) that maps to this traffic class. Required. Set in the VLAN tag of transmitted frames.

name.description: Human-readable name for this traffic class (e.g., "control", "video", "best-effort"). Optional.

description.description: Longer description of the traffic class purpose and contents. Optional.

preemption.description: IEEE 802.1Qbu preemption class (express or preemptable). express: cannot be interrupted. preemptable: can be interrupted by express traffic. Optional; default behavior is switch-specific.

traffic_class.description: Mapping between a traffic class number, a PCP value, and its QoS properties. Defines how frames are classified into priority queues for Qbv scheduling and CBS shaping.

items.description: Individual traffic class definition. One per TC number used in the network.

class.description: Individual traffic class definition. One per TC number used in the network.

count.description: Total number of traffic classes in use (1-8). Required. Must match the number of class child elements.

traffic_classes.description: Traffic class definitions for the network. Maps PCP values to traffic class numbers and optionally assigns preemption behavior. Used by Qbv schedules and CBS shapers to determine queue assignments.

motor.description: Reference to the motor that drives this transmission. Uses dotted path "comp_name.motor_name".

joint.description: Reference to the joint that this transmission drives. Uses the joint name.

reduction.description: Gear reduction ratio (motor_speed / joint_speed). Values greater than 1 indicate speed reduction / torque multiplication. For example, 100 means the motor turns 100 times for each joint revolution.

efficiency.description: Mechanical efficiency of the transmission as a fraction in [0, 1]. 1.0 = lossless, 0.9 = 90% efficient. Accounts for friction losses in gears, belts, and bearings.

backlash.description: Mechanical backlash (dead zone) at the joint output. Unit: typically "rad" for rotary or "m" for linear transmissions.

name.description: Unique name for this transmission (e.g., "shoulder_trans", "wheel_fl_belt"). Required.

type.description: Mechanical transmission type (simple, differential, belt, gear, planetary, cycloidal, harmonic, strain-wave, worm). Optional; defaults to simple if not specified.

encoder.description: Name reference to a joint-side encoder sensor. This is the encoder on the output side of the transmission (after the gearbox), providing higher-accuracy joint position feedback than the motor-side encoder. Optional — not all transmissions have output encoders.

visual.description: Name of the visual element containing this transmission's mesh for 3D highlighting.

mesh.description: GLTF mesh node name within the visual for 3D highlighting of this transmission.

transmission.description: Mechanical power transmission connecting a motor to a joint. Defines the gear reduction ratio, efficiency, and backlash for the mechanical coupling between motor output and joint input.

ref.description: Dotted reference path to the motor or joint (e.g., "shoulder_comp.shoulder_motor" or "shoulder_joint"). Required.

transmission_endpoint.description: Reference to a motor or joint endpoint in a transmission. Uses a dotted path "comp.element" to identify the target across components.

max-groups.description: Maximum number of ATS shaping groups the port hardware supports.

tsn_ats_capability.description: IEEE 802.1Qcr Asynchronous Traffic Shaping capability. Provides per-stream shaping without requiring time synchronization, useful for non-gPTP-capable devices.

max-sr-classes.description: Maximum number of SR (Stream Reservation) classes supported. Standard AVB defines 2 classes (A and B). Some hardware supports additional classes. Default: 2.

tsn_cbs_capability.description: IEEE 802.1Qav Credit-Based Shaper capability. Provides bandwidth reservation for AVB/TSN streams by limiting burst size via credit accumulation. Presence indicates CBS support. The max-sr-classes attribute specifies how many SR (Stream Reservation) classes the port hardware supports.

seq-encoding.description: Sequence number encoding format. Default: "r-tag" (IEEE 802.1CB R-TAG). Alternatives include "hsrp" for HSR/PRP sequence tags.

tsn_frer_capability.description: IEEE 802.1CB Frame Replication and Elimination for Reliability capability. Provides seamless redundancy by duplicating streams over disjoint paths and eliminating duplicates at the receiver.

hw-timestamping.description: Whether the port supports IEEE 802.1AS hardware timestamping for nanosecond-accurate clock synchronization. Default: false.

gm-capable.description: Whether the device behind this port can serve as gPTP grandmaster clock. Default: false.

tsn_gptp_capability.description: IEEE 802.1AS gPTP capability of a port. Indicates whether the port supports hardware timestamping and grandmaster election.

psfp.description: IEEE 802.1Qci Per-Stream Filtering and Policing capability.

frer.description: IEEE 802.1CB Frame Replication and Elimination for Reliability capability.

tsn_port_capabilities.description: TSN (Time-Sensitive Networking) capabilities of a port. Declares which IEEE 802.1 TSN sub-standards the port hardware supports. Used by network planners to determine valid schedule and stream placements.

min-fragment-size.description: Minimum preemptable fragment size in bytes. Preempted frames are split at fragment boundaries no smaller than this. Default: 64 bytes.

tsn_preemption_capability.description: IEEE 802.1Qbu/802.3br frame preemption capability. Allows express traffic to interrupt preemptable frames mid-transmission, reducing worst-case latency for high-priority traffic.

max-filters.description: Maximum number of stream filters the port hardware supports.

max-gates.description: Maximum number of stream gates (time-based ingress windows) the port hardware supports.

max-meters.description: Maximum number of flow meters for bandwidth policing the port hardware supports.

tsn_psfp_capability.description: IEEE 802.1Qci Per-Stream Filtering and Policing capability. Provides ingress filtering, rate policing, and stream identification for protecting against misbehaving talkers.

traffic-classes.description: Number of traffic classes (priority queues) supported by the port. Default: 8 (IEEE 802.1Q maximum).

hw-queues.description: Number of hardware transmit queues. Default: 8. May differ from traffic-classes if queue-to-TC mapping is used.

max-gcl-entries.description: Maximum number of Gate Control List entries the port hardware supports. Limits the complexity of Qbv schedules that can be programmed.

tsn_qbv_capability.description: IEEE 802.1Qbv Time-Aware Shaper capability of a port. Defines the port's gate scheduling hardware resources.

chain.description: Name of the outer/transport chain that carries the encapsulated traffic. Required.

hop.description: Device name of the hop on the outer chain where the tunnel ingress/egress occurs. Required.

protocol.description: Encapsulation protocol used for tunneling (etg-1700, ieee-1722, raw). Optional; defaults to the most appropriate protocol for the chain types involved.

tunnel.description: Tunnel configuration for carrying one chain's traffic encapsulated over another chain or link. Used for EtherCAT-over-TSN (IEEE 1722 encapsulation) or EtherCAT-over-EtherCAT (ETG.1700) scenarios.

pose.description: Pose of the visual relative to the component origin as "x y z roll pitch yaw" (meters and radians). Default: identity (no offset).

model.description: High-fidelity 3D model reference (GLB/glTF). Preferred over geometry when available.

geometry.description: Geometry primitive fallback for rendering when no 3D model is available.

material.description: Material properties (color, texture) for this visual. Overrides any material in the referenced model.

name.description: Unique name for this visual within the component. Required. Used for programmatic toggling and identification.

toggle.description: Toggle group name. Visuals sharing the same toggle group can be shown/hidden together at runtime (e.g., "leds", "covers").

visual.description: Visual representation of a component for rendering. Contains either a high-fidelity 3D model reference or a geometry primitive fallback (or both, where model is preferred and geometry is the fallback). Multiple visuals per component are allowed for multi-part rendering.

id.description: VLAN identifier (VID), range 1-4094. Required. Frames on this link/chain are tagged with this VID.

pcp.description: Priority Code Point (PCP), range 0-7. Maps to a traffic class for QoS. Higher values = higher priority. Optional; if omitted, PCP is determined by the traffic class mapping.

vlan.description: IEEE 802.1Q VLAN tag assignment for a link, chain, or spur. Segments network traffic into virtual LANs for isolation and QoS mapping.

min.description: Minimum input/output voltage in the specified unit.

max.description: Maximum input/output voltage in the specified unit.

voltage_capability.description: Port voltage capability. Text content is the nominal voltage. Attributes define the acceptable operating range. Used for power ports and powered Ethernet (PoDL).

type.description: Wheel type: "driven" (has motor), "steering" (steerable), "caster" (passive swivel), "omni" (omnidirectional rollers), "mecanum" (45-degree rollers). Optional.

wheel_surface.description: Wheel ground contact surface. Defines the tire/wheel geometry and friction properties for ground vehicles. The motor driving this wheel is defined separately; the wheel surface describes the contact interface with the ground.

items.description: Dotted port reference "comp_name.port_name" identifying an endpoint of this link. Exactly two required (one per end). Order does not matter for Ethernet links; for T1 links, PHY master/slave is set on the port, not here.

port.description: Dotted port reference "comp_name.port_name" identifying an endpoint of this link. Exactly two required (one per end). Order does not matter for Ethernet links; for T1 links, PHY master/slave is set on the port, not here.

vlan.description: VLAN tag assignment for traffic on this link.

macsec.description: Per-link MACsec policy override. If omitted, the network-wide macsec default applies.

ip.description: IP address assignment for this link in CIDR notation (e.g., "10.0.1.0/24"). Optional; used by runtime agents for interface configuration.

standard.description: PHY standard for the wired link (e.g., "100base-t1", "1000base-t", "25gbase-sr", "802.3dm"). Required. Must be compatible with both endpoint ports' capabilities.

wired.description: Wired point-to-point link physical layer definition. Contains exactly two port references (the two endpoints), plus optional VLAN, MACsec, and IP configuration.

items.description: Dotted antenna reference "comp_name.antenna_name" identifying an endpoint of this wireless link. Exactly two required.

antenna.description: Dotted antenna reference "comp_name.antenna_name" identifying an endpoint of this wireless link. Exactly two required.

ssid.description: WiFi SSID (network name) for WiFi links. Optional; only applicable when standard is an 802.11 variant.

standard.description: Wireless standard for this link (e.g., "802.11ax", "bluetooth-5.3", "lora-1.0.4"). Required. Must be compatible with both endpoint antennas' capabilities.

wireless.description: Wireless link physical layer definition. Contains exactly two antenna references (the two endpoints) and an optional SSID for WiFi links.

description.description: Human-readable description of the mesh network's purpose (e.g., "tactile skin sensors on left hand", "UWB positioning anchors").

items.description: A device participating in this mesh. At least one node should have role="coordinator" or role="border-router".

node.description: A device participating in this mesh. At least one node should have role="coordinator" or role="border-router".

name.description: Unique name for this mesh network (e.g., "hand-tactile-mesh", "uwb-anchors").

standard.description: PHY/MAC standard: 802.15.4 (Thread/Zigbee), bluetooth (BLE mesh), 802.11s (WiFi mesh), uwb, lora.

protocol.description: Higher-layer mesh routing protocol: Thread, Zigbee, BLE-mesh, OpenThread, 802.11s, LoRa-mesh. The protocol determines how nodes discover each other, form routes, and self-heal.

channel.description: RF channel or frequency band for the mesh (e.g., "15" for 802.15.4 channel 15, "2.4GHz"). Protocol-dependent.

pan-id.description: Personal Area Network ID for 802.15.4-based meshes (Thread, Zigbee). 16-bit hex value (e.g., "0x1234"). Not applicable for BLE mesh or WiFi mesh.

network-key-ref.description: Reference to the mesh network encryption key in an external key store. No actual keys in HCDF — only a reference for the agent to provision at runtime. Thread uses a 128-bit network key; BLE mesh uses NetKey/AppKey.

wireless_mesh.description: Wireless mesh network where nodes self-organize routing topology. Unlike buses (shared medium, no routing) and chains (fixed forwarding order), mesh networks discover paths dynamically and can self-heal when nodes join or leave. Use for Thread/802.15.4 sensor meshes, BLE mesh sensor arrays, UWB positioning networks, and WiFi mesh (802.11s). The HCDF declares mesh membership and protocol — the routing protocol handles topology autonomously at runtime.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this HCDF file describes. For a top-level robot: the robot's purpose and key characteristics (e.g., "CogniPilot humanoid mobile base research platform with dual 7-DOF arms and TSN backbone"). For a reusable sub-assembly: the module's function and interface (e.g., "7-DOF manipulator arm with HEBI X8-series SEA actuators, 802.3dm daisy-chain, and force/torque sensing at wrist"). Helps humans and AI agents understand the file's contents without parsing the full document.

author.description: Author or organization that created this HCDF file (e.g., "CogniPilot Foundation", "HEBI Robotics", "Jane Doe"). For composed systems, each included file retains its own author, allowing BOM tools to trace which vendor or team provided each sub-assembly. Optional.

license.description: License governing use of this HCDF file and the design it describes. Use SPDX license identifiers when possible (e.g., "Apache-2.0", "BSD-3-Clause", "MIT", "CERN-OHL-S-2.0" for open hardware, "LicenseRef-Proprietary" for closed designs). For composed systems, each included file can carry a different license, enabling mixed open-source and proprietary sub-assemblies. Optional.

url.description: Canonical URL for this HCDF file's project, documentation, or source repository (e.g., "https://github.com/CogniPilot/hcdformat"). Provides a stable reference for humans and tooling to find updates, issues, and related resources. Optional.

items.description: Component definitions (rigid bodies / hardware assemblies).
Kinematic joint connections between components.
Named joint groups for planning and control (e.g., left_arm, locomotion).

group.description: Named joint groups for planning and control (e.g., left_arm, locomotion).

state.description: Named kinematic states / poses (e.g., home, transport).

self-collision-disable.description: Component pairs to skip for self-collision checking.

network.description: Network definitions containing topology (links, buses, chains, meshes) and configuration (gPTP, TSN schedules, MACsec).

Global material definitions (color, surface friction, restitution).

name.description: Name of the robot, platform, or sub-assembly described by this file. Required. For top-level robot descriptions, this is the robot's model name (e.g., "cogni-humanoid-v2", "quadrotor-x500"). For reusable sub-assemblies, this is the module name (e.g., "7dof-arm", "stereo-head-v2", "hebi-x8-9"). When a file is imported via include, the include's name attribute provides the instance name (e.g., "left_arm"), while this attribute provides the module identity. Use lowercase-with-hyphens by convention.

version.description: HCDF schema version this file conforms to. Current: 1.0. Parsers should accept documents with the same MAJOR version and ignore unknown elements for forward compatibility.

body-frame.description: Body-frame axis convention for all pose values in this file. FLU (X-forward, Y-left, Z-up): common in ground robotics and humanoid platforms. FRD (X-forward, Y-right, Z-down): common in aerospace and flight controllers. All xyz and rpy values in this document are expressed in the specified body-frame convention. Optional; when omitted, consumers should not assume a default and must either infer from context or require explicit specification. Semantic rule: all files in an include chain must use the same body-frame.

world-frame.description: World-frame (navigation/map frame) axis convention for global coordinates. ENU (X-east, Y-north, Z-up): common in GIS, surveying, and ground robotics. NED (X-north, Y-east, Z-down): common in aerospace, marine navigation, and flight controllers. Determines how global poses, waypoints, and map-relative coordinates are interpreted. Independent of body-frame. Optional; when omitted, consumers should not assume a default. Semantic rule: all files in an include chain must use the same world-frame.
